parent_heading: 5. Configuration: How Principals Build Their Ensembles
heading: 5.1. The Configuration Problem
text: A principal entering an ecosystem with hundreds of available sub-agents faces a configuration problem. They do not yet know: - Which specializations are most relevant to their work - Which combinations work well together - Which sub-agents are highest quality - How their configuration should evolve over time This is similar to how a new yoga student facing hundreds of possible teachers doesn't know which twenty to pursue. The configuration emerges from a process, not a single decision.

parent_heading: 5. Configuration: How Principals Build Their Ensembles
heading: 5.2. Three Configuration Approaches
text: We identify three approaches with different strengths and trade-offs. Approach 1 — Self-directed. The principal browses the registry, selects sub-agents based on their self-assessment, and configures their composite agent. Strengths: Full agency. Principal develops deep knowledge of the registry. No external influence on selection. Weaknesses: Requires substantial self-knowledge. New practitioners often don't know what they need. May miss valuable specializations they hadn't considered. Approach 2 — AI-assisted recommendation. The composite agent (or a separate recommendation system) analyzes the principal's stated goals, work history, and preferences, then suggests an initial configuration. Principal modifies as needed. Strengths: Lower barrier to entry. Surfaces specializations the principal might not have considered. Faster initial setup. Weaknesses: Risk of recommendation bias. Algorithmic suggestions may converge toward popular configurations, reducing diversity. Principal may accept recommendations passively without learning the registry. Approach 3 — Mentor-guided. An experienced practitioner — ideally one who has developed their own composite practice — helps the principal think through their configuration. Strengths: Deep human judgment about what configurations work. Mentor knows what the principal's stated goals actually require. Builds relationship beyond the technology. Weaknesses: Requires access to a mentor. Mentor's perspective may bias selection. Doesn't scale automatically.

parent_heading: 5. Configuration: How Principals Build Their Ensembles
heading: 5.3. Hybrid Configuration
text: In practice, the best configuration process combines all three approaches: 1. Initial AI recommendation based on principal's stated profile generates a starting configuration 2. Self-directed exploration lets principal browse, adjust, add specializations they recognize as relevant 3. Mentor review (optional) provides experienced human perspective, especially for principals new to their field This three-step process echoes how human professional development actually works: training programs provide initial frameworks, self-directed learning fills gaps, and mentors provide deep guidance.

parent_heading: 5. Configuration: How Principals Build Their Ensembles
heading: 5.4. Configuration Evolution
text: A principal's configuration is not static. Specializations change over time: - New specializations enter their work as careers evolve - Old specializations become less relevant as priorities shift - New sub-agents become available in the registry - Old sub-agents may be deprecated The composite agent should help the principal monitor configuration health: - Which sub-agents are most used? - Which are rarely consulted? - Are there new sub-agents that match the principal's recent work patterns? Periodic configuration review (perhaps annually) keeps the composite agent aligned with the principal's evolving practice.

parent_heading: 5. Configuration: How Principals Build Their Ensembles
heading: 5.5. Templates and Starting Points
text: For many professions, common configurations exist. A new social law lawyer might benefit from a starting template: - General SGB IX sub-agent - General SGB XII sub-agent - Procedural law sub-agent - BSG case law sub-agent - General drafting sub-agent This template provides a reasonable starting configuration. The practitioner then specializes by adding sub-agents specific to their developing practice (e.g., disability rights specifically, or social welfare for elderly clients, or working with mental health conditions). Templates are starting points, not endpoints. Every practitioner modifies their configuration over time. --- --- Смотрите также: - 258-5-configuration-how-principals-build-their-ensembl - 07-economics-combinatorial - 03-what-makes-csa - 11-open-questions --- Похожие документы: - 258-5-configuration-how-principals-build-their-ensembl (сходство 0.87) - 258-5-configuration-how-principals-build-their-ensembl (сходство 0.83) - 07-economics-combinatorial (сходство 0.21)

heading: Содержание
text: - 6. Coordination and Disagreement Resolution - 6.1. Why Sub-Agents Disagree - 6.2. Surfacing Versus Resolving - 6.3. Presentation of Disagreement - 6.4. When the Principal Cannot Decide - 6.5. Learning from Resolution --- > Источник: MHTML‑снимок Вакансии в Anthropic по кластерам - Claude (корень репозитория). Раздел диалога — paper «Composite Skills Agents».

heading: 6.1. Why Sub-Agents Disagree
text: Disagreement is normal and informative in composite configurations. Several common causes: Different specialization frames. A sub-agent on legal strategy may recommend X; a sub-agent on case management may recommend Y. Each is correct within its frame. Different evidence weights. A sub-agent may weight recent case law heavily; another may weight long-established methodology more. Both are reasonable choices that produce different recommendations. Different optimization targets. A sub-agent may optimize for client outcomes; another for procedural correctness. These goals can conflict. Different boundary handling. Some questions fall on the boundary between specializations. Each sub-agent may interpret the boundary differently. These disagreements often contain the most important information the principal needs. A composite agent that resolved them silently would hide what the principal most needs to see.

heading: 6.2. Surfacing Versus Resolving
text: A central architectural commitment: the composite agent surfaces disagreements; it does not resolve them. Surfacing means: presenting each sub-agent's recommendation clearly, with reasoning, so the principal can see what each specialization contributes. Not resolving means: the composite agent does not vote, average, or arbitrate between sub-agents. The principal makes the call. This is critical because: - Algorithmic resolution would impose hidden judgment on the principal - Resolution loses information that the disagreement contains - The principal's authority over their work depends on access to underlying disagreement - Profession-level differences in approach should remain visible, not be smoothed away

heading: 6.3. Presentation of Disagreement
text: How disagreement is presented matters. Some patterns work well: Pattern 1 — Side-by-side recommendation. "Sub-agent A recommends X with reasoning [...]; sub-agent B recommends Y with reasoning [...]." Principal sees both directly. Pattern 2 — Common ground first. "Both sub-agents agree that [base position]. They differ on [specific issue]: A says X; B says Y." Helps principal see what is settled and what is contested. Pattern 3 — Compatibility analysis. "A's recommendation may produce result X. B's recommendation may produce result Y. The choice depends on whether you prioritize Z." Helps principal see what the disagreement actually means for outcomes. Pattern 4 — Resolution suggestion (without execution). "If you favor X, here's how A's recommendation can be implemented. If you favor Y, here's how B's." Principal still chooses; the agent helps with implementation of the chosen direction. What does not work: averaging, voting, "balanced" outputs that mask the underlying disagreement. These appear neutral but actually impose hidden judgment.

heading: 6.4. When the Principal Cannot Decide
text: Sometimes the principal genuinely doesn't know which recommendation to follow. The composite agent has options: Escalation to mentor. If the principal has access to a human mentor, the composite agent can structure the disagreement for mentor review. Deferred decision. Some decisions can be deferred — the agent helps the principal collect more information before committing. Risk-weighted default. If one recommendation is more conservative (smaller commitment, easier reversal), the composite agent can suggest defaulting to that while gathering more information. Acknowledged uncertainty. Sometimes the right output is: "You face a genuine professional judgment call here. Here's what each sub-agent recommends; here's what's at stake; this is a moment where your accumulated experience matters." The composite agent acknowledging the limits of agent guidance is itself valuable.

heading: 6.5. Learning from Resolution
text: How the principal resolves disagreements — over time — is informative. The composite agent can track: - Which sub-agents the principal tends to follow when there's disagreement - Which contexts predict which choice - Whether the principal's choices show evolving patterns This information should be available to the principal (their own pattern of decisions over time) but not used to hide future disagreements. The point is not to predict and pre-resolve; the point is to give the principal insight into their own practice. --- --- Смотрите также: - 259-6-coordination-and-disagreement-resolution - 03-what-makes-csa - 256-3-what-makes-a-composite-skills-agent - 05-configuration-ensembles --- Похожие документы: - 259-6-coordination-and-disagreement-resolution (сходство 0.88) - 259-6-coordination-and-disagreement-resolution (сходство 0.84) - 03-what-makes-csa (сходство 0.21)

heading: Содержание
text: - 7. Economics of Combinatorial Replication - 7.1. Cost Structure - 7.2. Combinatorial Value - 7.3. Investment Strategy - 7.4. Funding Models for Sub-Agent Development - 7.5. Sustainable Economics for the SGB Pilot --- > Источник: MHTML‑снимок Вакансии в Anthropic по кластерам - Claude (корень репозитория). Раздел диалога — paper «Composite Skills Agents».

heading: 7.1. Cost Structure
text: Sub-agent development: substantial fixed cost per sub-agent. A high-quality narrow-specialist sub-agent might cost €50,000- €500,000 to build, depending on knowledge base depth. Sub-agent maintenance: moderate ongoing cost. Perhaps 10-20% of build cost annually for keeping the knowledge base current. Coordinator development: substantial one-time cost for the coordinator infrastructure. Once built, serves all configurations. Coordinator operation: low marginal cost per principal. LLM API costs scale with use, but the coordinator itself doesn't require per-principal customization beyond configuration. Configuration management: low per-principal cost. Adding sub-agents to a configuration is essentially free; the sub-agents already exist. This produces interesting unit economics. If an ecosystem has 500 sub-agents and 10,000 principals, the ratio of sub-agent fixed cost to total user value is highly favorable.

heading: 7.2. Combinatorial Value
text: The number of possible configurations grows combinatorially: - 100 sub-agents, choose 15: 250 billion possible configurations - 500 sub-agents, choose 20: astronomical In practice, configurations cluster around common patterns. But even within any profession, there's room for substantial diversity. Each principal's configuration genuinely reflects their distinctive practice. The combinatorial value is the central economic insight: a relatively small fixed investment in sub-agent development unlocks an essentially unlimited space of unique principal configurations.

heading: 7.3. Investment Strategy
text: For an ecosystem (like OKWF) building this infrastructure, the investment strategy follows from the economics: Phase 1: Build a small set of broadly applicable sub-agents (perhaps 20-50 covering core specializations of one profession). This requires substantial upfront investment but enables initial configurations. Phase 2: Expand to more specialized sub-agents based on observed configuration gaps. As principals use the system, their requests for specializations not yet available drive priority for new sub-agent development. Phase 3: Federation with external sub-agent providers. Once the registry framework is established, sub-agents from other organizations can be integrated, accelerating coverage. Phase 4: Cross-profession expansion. Sub-agents developed for one profession may apply to adjacent professions. Methodology sub-agents particularly cross professional boundaries. This phasing parallels how academic libraries develop: starting with core texts, expanding to specialized works, federating with other libraries, and enabling cross-disciplinary access.

heading: 7.4. Funding Models for Sub-Agent Development
text: Several funding models are viable: Foundation-funded, public registry. Most aligned with OKWF mission. Sub-agents developed through grants, available freely. Practitioner-funded, cooperative ownership. Practitioners in a profession collectively fund sub-agent development; they own the registry collectively. Sponsorship-based. Foundations or institutions sponsor specific sub-agents (e.g., disability rights organizations sponsor sub-agents for disability-related specializations). Commercial provider, open standards. Companies build sub-agents commercially but follow open standards allowing integration. Mixed quality control, market-driven prioritization. Hybrid. Most realistic for early-stage ecosystem. Foundation funds core infrastructure, sponsors fund priority sub-agents, practitioners contribute to specific narrow sub-agents through volunteer expert work. For OKWF specifically, the hybrid approach plus eventual cooperative ownership seems most aligned with mission.

heading: 7.5. Sustainable Economics for the SGB Pilot
text: Applied to the SGB Advocate Colleague pilot from Professional Colleague Agents v1.0: Instead of building one large profession-wide agent for German social law, build: - 20-30 narrow sub-agents covering core SGB specializations - A coordinator that practitioners configure individually - Templates for common practice patterns (general advocacy, disability rights, elderly care, mental health, child welfare) Estimated investment: - 20 sub-agents at €50K average: €1,000,000 - Coordinator development: €300,000 - First-year operations: €200,000 - Total Year 1: €1.5 million This is higher than the original estimate (€430K) but produces substantially more capability. Practitioners get composite configurations matching their specific practice, not a generic SGB agent. For 1,000 practitioners over three years, this comes to approximately €500/practitioner/year — feasible for foundation funding or modest practitioner contribution. --- --- Смотрите также: - 260-7-economics-of-combinatorial-replication - 09-okwf-integration - 05-economics-replication - 262-9-integration-with-okwf-infrastructure --- Похожие документы: - 260-7-economics-of-combinatorial-replication (сходство 0.90) - 260-7-economics-of-combinatorial-replication (сходство 0.87) - 05-economics-replication (сходство 0.24)

text: - 8. Seven Domains of Application - 8.1. Domain 1 — Music Education and Performance - 8.2. Domain 2 — Visual and Creative Arts - 8.3. Domain 3 — Specialized Legal Practice - 8.4. Domain 4 — Medical Practice with Multiple Specialties - 8.5. Domain 5 — Technical Architecture and Engineering - 8.6. Domain 6 — Academic Research and Cross-Disciplinary Work - 8.7. Domain 7 — Therapeutic Practice and Mental Health - 8.8. Pattern Across Domains --- > Источник: MHTML‑снимок Вакансии в Anthropic по кластерам - Claude (корень репозитория). Раздел диалога — paper «Composite Skills Agents».

heading: 8.1. Domain 1 — Music Education and Performance
text: Sub-agent specializations might include: Classical composition, jazz improvisation, electronic music production, specific instruments (piano, violin, voice, etc.), genres (folk traditions, contemporary, baroque), pedagogy (children, adults, beginners, advanced), technical skills (music theory, ear training, sight-reading), accompanying disciplines (audio engineering, music history, ethnomusicology). Typical configurations: A jazz pianist who teaches has different needs from a classical violinist, who has different needs from an electronic music producer. Each builds their own configuration from the available sub-agents. Existing analog: This pattern is already embodied in how musicians actually train — studying with multiple teachers in different specialties — but no AI infrastructure currently supports it.

heading: 8.2. Domain 2 — Visual and Creative Arts
text: Sub-agent specializations might include: Drawing fundamentals, painting techniques, digital illustration, animation, sculpture, photography, color theory, composition, art history (by period or movement), specific tools (Adobe Creative Suite, Procreate, Blender), business skills (gallery work, commission management, intellectual property). Typical configurations: A digital illustrator who also does traditional sketching and runs a small business has very different needs from a fine arts painter focused on gallery representation. Bridge to existing infrastructure: Many artists already combine multiple educational sources (online courses, traditional training, peer learning). Composite agents formalize and augment this practice.

heading: 8.3. Domain 3 — Specialized Legal Practice
text: Sub-agent specializations might include: Specific area of law (social, criminal, family, immigration), procedural expertise (specific courts, jurisdictions), client population (elderly, disabled, immigrants, children), language (German, English, Russian, etc.), technical skills (drafting, oral advocacy, negotiation), supporting fields (medical understanding, psychological assessment). Typical configurations: A lawyer specializing in disability rights for migrants speaks both German and the migrant's language and has medical understanding for assessing claims — a combination requiring multiple sub-agents. Practical pilot: This is the SGB Advocate Colleague case discussed in detail in Section 7.5.

heading: 8.4. Domain 4 — Medical Practice with Multiple Specialties
text: Sub-agent specializations might include: Specific organ systems, types of conditions (acute, chronic, infectious, autoimmune), patient populations (pediatric, geriatric, prenatal), specialized procedures, diagnostic interpretation (radiology, lab work, pathology), specific subspecialties (rheumatology, immunology, infectious diseases). Typical configurations: A primary care physician working in a rural clinic with elderly patients faces different challenges from a specialist working in tertiary care. Even within specialties, working with specific patient populations requires different configurations. Critical caveat: Medical configurations require especially rigorous quality assurance and explicit boundaries around what the composite agent can support.

heading: 8.5. Domain 5 — Technical Architecture and Engineering
text: Sub-agent specializations might include: Programming languages, architecture patterns, deployment platforms, security disciplines, performance engineering, testing approaches, specific application domains (fintech, medtech, e-commerce, infrastructure), compliance regimes. Typical configurations: An engineer specializing in high-performance distributed systems for medical applications needs specific combinations: distributed systems, performance engineering, plus medical compliance, plus security requirements specific to healthcare. Bridge to existing tools: Many engineering teams already informally combine specialist resources (Stack Overflow, language-specific documentation, specialist consultants). Composite agents formalize this.

heading: 8.6. Domain 6 — Academic Research and Cross-Disciplinary Work
text: Sub-agent specializations might include: Specific methodologies (quantitative, qualitative, mixed methods), research traditions, statistical approaches, computational methods, writing conventions for specific journals or fields, literature search across databases, ethical frameworks. Typical configurations: A computational biologist working on epidemiology with humanities co-authors faces unique configuration needs combining biology, computer science, epidemiological methods, and humanities perspectives. Profession evolution: Academic research increasingly bridges fields. Composite agents support this trend rather than fighting it.

heading: 8.7. Domain 7 — Therapeutic Practice and Mental Health
text: Sub-agent specializations might include: Therapeutic modalities (CBT, somatic experiencing, narrative therapy, gestalt), specific conditions (trauma, addiction, eating disorders, mood disorders), client populations (children, adolescents, elderly, couples, groups), supporting frameworks (neuroscience, attachment theory, family systems), cultural competencies, specific skills (crisis intervention, group facilitation). Typical configurations: A therapist working with adolescents who have eating disorders in immigrant families requires specific combinations spanning therapeutic modalities, developmental psychology, eating disorder specifics, and cultural competency. Critical caveat: Mental health practice requires especially careful attention to where AI support is appropriate (research, documentation, knowledge lookup) versus where it is not (clinical judgment, therapeutic relationship). Composite agents operate strictly in the support role.

heading: 8.8. Pattern Across Domains
text: What unites these domains: - Practice is deeply specialized, but specialization combines rather than replaces general profession competency - Practitioners typically span 10-30 distinct specializations - Each specialization is shared with other practitioners but configurations are individually distinctive - Existing professional development already follows this pattern; composite agents formalize and enable it more broadly The pattern is general. We expect Composite Skills Agents to become standard infrastructure across most skilled professions within 5-10 years. --- --- Смотрите также: - 261-8-seven-domains-of-application - 02-twenty-one-teachers-pattern - 255-2-the-twenty-one-teachers-pattern - 07-application-domains --- Похожие документы: - 261-8-seven-domains-of-application (сходство 0.91) - 261-8-seven-domains-of-application (сходство 0.89) - 02-twenty-one-teachers-pattern (сходство 0.22)

Connection to Representative Agents - 9.5. Refined OKWF Rollout Plan - 9.6. Practical Implication for Pilot Design --- > Источник: MHTML‑снимок Вакансии в Anthropic по кластерам - Claude (корень репозитория). Раздел диалога — paper «Composite Skills Agents».

heading: 9.1. Connection to Pattern Library
text: The Nautilus pattern library architecture (public patterns, private instances, anonymization pipeline) provides foundational support for the sub-agent registry. Patterns become source material for sub-agents. Anonymized practitioner contributions flow into pattern library, which informs sub-agent updates. The connection is not just analogical. The same infrastructure serves both: - Pattern library: knowledge fragments accessible to humans for review - Sub-agent registry: knowledge fragments operationalized into AI assistants A given knowledge artifact can serve both functions.

heading: 9.2. Connection to Guild Structure
text: OKWF's guild structure (groups of practitioners by professional area) provides natural organization for sub-agent development. Each guild can: - Identify priority sub-agents for their area - Contribute expert curation - Test sub-agents in actual practice - Maintain quality standards - Develop common configuration templates Guilds become both consumers and producers of composite infrastructure.

heading: 9.3. Connection to Double-Triangle Architecture
text: In the Double-Triangle Architecture, each Node (human practitioner) has lower-triangle assistants supporting their work. Composite Skills Agents formalize what lower-triangle assistants typically contain: not one undifferentiated assistant but a coordinated configuration of narrow specialists. The composite agent itself is the orchestrator of the lower triangle. The sub-agents in the configuration are the actual "lower triangle assistants" doing specialized work. This refines the Double-Triangle model: the lower triangle is not a flat set of assistants but a coordinated ensemble.

heading: 9.4. Connection to Representative Agents
text: Composite Skills Agents and Representative Agents work together naturally: - The composite agent supports the practitioner's work - The representative agent supports the practitioner's external position A mature practitioner has both: composite for daily work, representative for opportunity navigation. They share underlying profile information about the practitioner but operate in different layers.

heading: 9.5. Refined OKWF Rollout Plan
text: Earlier papers proposed sequencing of OKWF deployment. With Composite Skills Agents recognized, the sequence refines to: Phase 0 (Months 1-12): Foundation establishment, initial sub-agent set for one profession (perhaps SGB social law). Build 20-30 narrow sub-agents plus coordinator. Pilot with 5-10 practitioners. Phase 1 (Year 2): Expand sub-agent set, scale practitioners to 100-500. Establish pattern library connection, anonymization pipeline. Begin contribution flow back into sub-agent updates. Phase 2 (Years 2-3): Expand to second profession (perhaps educational support for disabled students, maintaining thematic connection). Cross-profession integration of methodology sub-agents. Phase 3 (Years 3-5): Multiple professions, federated sub-agent registry, mature configuration management. Begin adding Representative Agent layer for principals who want it. Phase 4 (Year 5+): Multi-language, multi-jurisdiction expansion. Open registry with multiple curating organizations. This plan is more realistic than the earlier proposal because it matches how skilled practitioners actually develop and what they actually need.

heading: 9.6. Practical Implication for Pilot Design
text: For the SGB Advocate Colleague pilot specifically: Original plan: Build one comprehensive Professional Colleague Agent for German social law. Refined plan: Build a sub-agent registry covering 20-30 narrow SGB specializations, plus a coordinator that practitioners configure individually. Cost increases (€1.5M vs €430K for Year 1), but produces a genuinely useful infrastructure that scales to multiple adjacent legal domains and supports practitioners' actual diverse practices rather than forcing one-size-fits-all generalization. This is a better proposal for foundation funders even at higher cost, because it demonstrates a more sophisticated understanding of how skilled practice works and produces infrastructure with broader applicability. --- --- Смотрите также: - 262-9-integration-with-okwf-infrastructure - 13-closing - 07-economics-combinatorial - 04-sub-agent-registry --- Похожие документы: - 262-9-integration-with-okwf-infrastructure (сходство 0.89) - 262-9-integration-with-okwf-infrastructure (сходство 0.88) - 13-closing (сходство 0.24)

Risk: Coordinator Bias - 10.5. Risk: Quality Variation Across Sub-Agents - 10.6. Risk: Profession Fragmentation - 10.7. Risk: Hidden Dependencies Among Sub-Agents - 10.8. Risk: Configuration as Competitive Advantage Hoarding --- > Источник: MHTML‑снимок Вакансии в Anthropic по кластерам - Claude (корень репозитория). Раздел диалога — paper «Composite Skills Agents».

heading: 10.1. Risk: Configuration Paralysis
text: Scenario: Faced with hundreds of available sub-agents, the principal cannot decide which to include. Configuration becomes overwhelming task. Principal gives up and uses generic Professional Colleague Agent instead, missing the value of composite. Mitigations: - Strong starting templates for common professional patterns - AI-assisted recommendation as default (with override) - Limit on initial configuration size (start with 5-10, grow over time) - Mentor connection for complex configuration decisions

heading: 10.2. Risk: Configuration Echo Chamber
text: Scenario: Principal selects sub-agents that confirm their existing approach, missing specializations that would challenge or expand their practice. Composite becomes a sophisticated form of confirmation bias. Mitigations: - Recommendation system explicitly suggests "growth-edge" sub-agents outside principal's current configuration - Regular configuration reviews with prompts to consider unfamiliar specializations - Mentor input that intentionally surfaces alternative perspectives - Public visibility of how others in the same profession configure differently

heading: 10.3. Risk: Sub-Agent Dependency
text: Scenario: Principal becomes dependent on a specific sub-agent that is later deprecated or significantly changed. Their practice disrupts when configuration needs to change. Mitigations: - Open standards make sub-agents portable and replaceable - Multiple sub-agents covering same specialization (compete on quality, not lock-in) - Notification when sub-agent will be deprecated, with migration paths - Configuration export so principal can move between coordinator providers

heading: 10.4. Risk: Coordinator Bias
text: Scenario: The coordinator (general infrastructure managing configurations) develops biases in routing or synthesis that systematically favor some sub-agents over others. Practice patterns shift in ways principals didn't choose. Mitigations: - Open coordinator architecture, with multiple coordinator options - Transparent routing logic (principal can see why sub-agent was selected) - Auditable behavior over time - Coordinator evaluation as part of platform governance

heading: 10.5. Risk: Quality Variation Across Sub-Agents
text: Scenario: Sub-agent registry includes high-quality and low-quality sub-agents. Principals using lower-quality sub-agents in their configurations get poorer support without realizing it. Mitigations: - Visible quality indicators on sub-agents - Curation standards for inclusion in main registry - User feedback loops affecting visibility - Federated curation allowing principals to choose which curators they trust

heading: 10.6. Risk: Profession Fragmentation
text: Scenario: As composite configurations diversify, professional practice fragments. Practitioners working on similar problems develop incompatible approaches because they used different sub-agent configurations. Professional coherence weakens. Mitigations: - Sub-agents share common foundational knowledge (so configurations diverge from a common base) - Guild structure maintains profession-level community across diverse configurations - Cross-configuration patterns in shared pattern library - Recognition that some fragmentation is healthy diversification versus unhealthy disconnection

heading: 10.7. Risk: Hidden Dependencies Among Sub-Agents
text: Scenario: A configuration appears to work well, but its output depends on subtle interactions between specific sub-agents. When one sub-agent updates, the configuration's output changes in ways neither principal nor coordinator anticipated. Mitigations: - Version pinning (configurations specify exact sub-agent versions, opt into updates) - Compatibility testing when sub-agents update - Notification of significant behavioral changes in sub-agents - Configuration testing against past examples to detect drift

heading: 10.8. Risk: Configuration as Competitive Advantage Hoarding
text: Scenario: Principals discover that specific configurations are particularly effective. They keep these configurations secret as competitive advantage. Configuration knowledge becomes hoarded rather than shared, limiting benefit to broader practice. Mitigations: - Default openness about configurations (shared by default, opt-in to keep private) - Anonymized configuration patterns published in pattern library - Recognition for sharing effective configurations - Cooperative ownership models that align individual benefit with collective improvement --- --- Смотрите также: - 263-10-risks-specific-to-composite-architectures - 09-okwf-integration - 11-open-questions - 04-sub-agent-registry --- Похожие документы: - 263-10-risks-specific-to-composite-architectures (сходство 0.90) - 263-10-risks-specific-to-composite-architectures (сходство 0.86) - 09-okwf-integration (сходство 0.20)

heading: 11. Open Questions
text: > [!TIP] > Документ содержит практические рекомендации и лучшие практики. > > Источник: MHTML‑снимок Вакансии в Anthropic по кластерам - Claude (корень репозитория). Раздел диалога — paper «Composite Skills Agents». --- > Источник: MHTML‑снимок Вакансии в Anthropic по кластерам - Claude (корень репозитория). Раздел диалога — paper «Composite Skills Agents».

heading: 11.1. Granularity Questions
text: How narrow should sub-agents be? Too narrow and configurations become unmanageable; too broad and the composite pattern collapses back to Professional Colleague Agent. What's the right granularity for different professions? Likely varies — music might support finer granularity than law. Empirical question requiring deployment.

heading: 11.2. Configuration Stability
text: How often should principals revise configurations? Too often and they spend more time configuring than working; too rarely and configurations drift from current practice. What signals suggest configuration revision is needed? Likely some combination of: significant change in principal's work, new sub-agents available, observed gaps in current configuration, explicit annual review.

heading: 11.3. Cross-Professional Configurations
text: When practitioners genuinely span professions (lawyer who is also social worker, doctor who is also researcher), can their configuration span sub-agents from multiple professions? How does the coordinator handle this? This case is increasingly common as work becomes more interdisciplinary.

heading: 11.4. Configuration Evolution at Scale
text: If 10,000 practitioners use composite agents, what happens across the population? Do configurations cluster around patterns? Does this clustering match natural sub-specialties or create artificial groupings? Empirical study would inform whether the architecture supports or constrains professional evolution. Sub-agents developed primarily in one cultural or linguistic context may not serve well in others. How does the registry handle cultural variation? Multiple parallel sub-agents for different contexts? Configurable cultural overlays?

heading: 11.6. Liability for Composite Outputs
text: When a composite agent's output (drawing on multiple sub-agents) contains errors, who bears responsibility? The principal who used it? The coordinator? Each sub-agent that contributed? The platform? Legal frameworks for traditional Professional Colleague Agents are nascent; composite versions add complexity.

heading: 11.7. The "Emergence" Question
text: When many sub-agents combine in a configuration, do useful properties emerge that weren't present in any individual sub-agent? Or do composite outputs simply average their contributions? Theoretically interesting question with practical implications for whether composite is genuinely more than the sum of parts.

heading: 11.8. Transition from Composite to Representative
text: As principals develop sophisticated composite configurations, when (if ever) does it make sense to graduate to a Representative Agent (Type 4) with truly individual specialization? Or do composites serve indefinitely? The pattern from human mastery suggests configurations evolve indefinitely; "graduation" may not be the right metaphor. --- --- Смотрите также: - 264-11-open-questions - 12-call-for-collaboration - 09-okwf-integration - 03-what-makes-csa --- Похожие документы: - 264-11-open-questions (сходство 0.89) - 264-11-open-questions (сходство 0.86) - 12-call-for-collaboration (сходство 0.22)

heading: 12. Call for Collaboration
text: > > Источник: MHTML‑снимок Вакансии в Anthropic по кластерам - Claude (корень репозитория). Раздел диалога — paper «Composite Skills Agents». --- > Источник: MHTML‑снимок Вакансии в Anthropic по кластерам - Claude (корень репозитория). Раздел диалога — paper «Composite Skills Agents».

heading: 12. Call for Collaboration
text: This paper opens questions that require multi-stakeholder engagement. Tell us how your practice combines specializations. What configuration of sub-agents would actually serve your work? What would make composite agents valuable versus merely complex? Pilot participation invited, particularly for the SGB Advocate Colleague pilot. Sub-agent registry development needs curated knowledge across many narrow specializations. Domain experts willing to help develop sub-agents in their specialties — particularly compensated through foundation funding — would substantially accelerate deployment. Coordinator infrastructure is substantial engineering work. Open-source contributions to reference implementations welcome. Particular needs: - Sub-agent registry standards and protocols - Configuration management interfaces - Disagreement detection and presentation systems - Audit and observability tools Empirical studies of composite configuration patterns would illuminate questions raised in Section 11. Particular value in: - Configuration clustering analysis - Effectiveness comparison: composite vs. monolithic agents - Long-term effects on professional development - Cross-cultural deployment outcomes

heading: 12.5. For Foundation Funders
text: Composite Skills Agents represent higher-leverage infrastructure investment than monolithic Professional Colleague Agents: - More flexible support across diverse practitioner needs - More scalable across adjacent professions - More aligned with how mastery actually develops - More resilient to changes in any single component Funding sub-agent development is investing in foundational infrastructure that supports many configurations across many practitioners.

heading: 12.6. For Policymakers
text: Regulatory frameworks for AI in professional practice will need to address composite architectures specifically. Questions about liability, professional responsibility, and quality standards apply differently when many specialized sub-agents contribute to outputs. Engagement during regulatory development helps ensure frameworks match actual technology architecture. --- --- Смотрите также: - 265-12-call-for-collaboration - 222-11-call-for-collaboration - 11-open-questions - 11-call-for-collaboration --- Похожие документы: - 265-12-call-for-collaboration (сходство 0.85) - 265-12-call-for-collaboration (сходство 0.82) - 11-call-for-collaboration (сходство 0.30)

heading: 13. Closing
text: Mastery in skilled work has always been composite. The yoga master who studied with twenty teachers did not invent his practice from nothing; he combined what he learned from specialists. The musician, the lawyer, the physician, the architect, the therapist — all develop their distinctive practice by combining individually general skills into individually distinctive configurations. What is new is the possibility of AI infrastructure that mirrors this structure. Until now, AI support for skilled work has been either too generic (one assistant for everyone) or too custom (unique development per principal). Neither matches how practitioners actually develop and operate. Composite Skills Agents fill this gap. They make the twenty-teacher pattern accessible at scale: any practitioner can configure their own ensemble of specialist sub-agents, creating support that matches their actual practice rather than forcing them into pre-defined categories. This recognition changes the practical strategy for AI deployment in professions. Building monolithic profession-wide agents serves narrow purposes well but cannot serve the more common case of practitioners with distinctive specialized configurations. Building uniquely customized agents serves deep representational needs but cannot scale. Building composite infrastructure — registry of narrow sub-agents plus configurable coordinators — serves the broad middle where most skilled practice actually happens. For the Open Knowledge Work Foundation specifically, composite infrastructure provides: - More flexible support for diverse practitioner needs - Stronger alignment with mission of empowering distinctive practitioners - Better economic structure (combinatorial leverage of fixed investment) - More natural connection to existing pattern library architecture - Clearer migration path from initial pilot to mature ecosystem This is the architecture skilled work has needed. The question is whether we build it carefully — with open standards, foundation governance, quality curation, and practitioner ownership — or whether we let it emerge through commercial dynamics that favor concentration over distribution.

heading: Acknowledgments
text: The Composite Skills Agent concept emerged from dialogue with Claude (Anthropic) on April 26, 2026. The crystallizing contribution was the user's observation that the binary view between Professional Colleague Agent (Type 1) and Representative Agent (Type 4) missed the most common middle case — practitioners whose distinctive value comes from combining individually general specializations. The metaphor of the Indian yoga master who studied with twenty teachers, drawn from a journalist's interview the user remembered, provides the central illustration that makes the pattern clear. The user retained editorial authority. All claims, errors, and specific architectural proposals are author's responsibility. This paper is the seventh in the sequence developed across this extended collaboration: 1. Nautilus Portal Protocol v1.1 2. Review Methodology v1.0 3. Double-Triangle Architecture v1.0 4. Open Knowledge Work Foundation Concept Document v1.0 5. Representative Agent Layer v1.0 6. Professional Colleague Agents v1.0 7. Composite Skills Agent v1.0 (this paper) Together these papers form an architectural vision for AI-supported knowledge work that respects how mastery develops, how professions function, and how human dignity is preserved when AI augments human practice. ---

heading: Foundational Sources for Composite Architecture
text: - Anderson, J. R. (1983). The Architecture of Cognition. Harvard University Press. (On modular cognitive architecture.) - Minsky, M. (1986). The Society of Mind. Simon & Schuster. (On cognition as ensemble of specialized agents.) - Csikszentmihalyi, M. (1997). Creativity: Flow and the Psychology of Discovery and Invention. Harper. (On how expertise develops through specialization combined with synthesis.)

heading: Claude hat geantwortet: Это очень тонкое и важное наблюдение.
text: > [!IMPORTANT] > Ключевой документ для понимания архитектуры. Рекомендуется прочитать в первую очередь. > > Источник: MHTML‑снимок Вакансии в Anthropic по кластерам - Claude (корень репозитория). Раздел диалога — 21 учитель индийского йога — спектр между Professional Colleague и Representative Agent. --- > Источник: MHTML‑снимок Вакансии в Anthropic по кластерам - Claude (корень репозитория). Раздел диалога — 21 учитель индийского йога — спектр между Professional Colleague и Representative Agent. Claude hat geantwortet: Это очень тонкое и важное наблюдение. Это очень тонкое и важное наблюдение. Вы заметили нечто, что в шестой статье было упрощено — будто между Профессиональным Коллегой-Агентом (стандартизированная профессия) и Представительским Агентом (уникальный индивидум) существует чистый разрыв. На самом деле там целый спектр. Дайте мне разложить это аккуратно. Метафора с индийским йогом — точная и важная История, которую вы привели, имеет глубокий смысл. Двадцать один учитель, каждый со своей узкой специализацией. Двадцать первый из них синтезирует уникальную комбинацию из учений первых двадцати. И эта комбинация именно его — не его учителей по отдельности, и не «среднего йога». Это не редкая структура. Это базовая структура мастерства в любой сложной области. Музыкант, который учился у пианиста, у композитора, у дирижёра, у звукоинженера, у джазового импровизатора — становится уникальным именно через комбинацию, не через одно из этих направлений. Художник, который изучал классическую живопись, цифровую анимацию, скульптуру, графический дизайн, фотографию — формирует свой стиль на пересечениях. Юрист, который работал в корпоративном праве, потом в правах потребителей, потом в технологическом праве — приносит уникальную перспективу, которая не существует ни у одного из его предыдущих наставников. Этот паттерн универсален в человеческой профессиональной деятельности. И существующая шестая статья его не уловила. Что я пропустил в предыдущей статье В шестой статье я провёл бинарное различение: Тип 1 (Профессиональный Коллега) — стандартизованная профессия, один шаблон для всех

parent_heading: Claude hat geantwortet: Это очень тонкое и важное наблюдение.
heading: Claude hat geantwortet: Это очень тонкое и важное наблюдение.
text: Тип 4 (Представительский Агент) — уникальный индивидуум, агент для одного человека Между ними — пропасть. Будто ничего нет в середине. Но реальный мир работает иначе. Большинство профессионалов не на полюсах. Они в промежуточной зоне: имеют профессиональную базу плюс уникальный набор специализаций, который делает их особенными среди коллег по профессии. Учитель литературы, который специализируется на современной поэзии и феминистских интерпретациях. Не «уникальный единственный в мире» (это слишком высокая планка), но и не «типичный учитель литературы». Что-то среднее. Юрист по социальному праву, который специализируется на случаях, связанных с инвалидностью, и при этом имеет техническое образование. Не уникальный, но и не типичный. Эта средняя зона — где находится подавляющее большинство профессионалов. И там тоже нужны AI-коллеги, но другого типа. Что вы предлагаете — Композитный Агент Назову это пока Composite Skills Agent или Композитный Агент Навыков. Возможно, лучшее название найдётся, но суть в этом. Принципал имеет набор узких специализаций — допустим, восемь, или двенадцать, или двадцать. Не одну широкую профессию. Не уникальную идентичность. Набор. Композитный Агент должен: Подключаться к множеству узкоспециализированных «под-агентов» Каждый под-агент — глубокий эксперт в одной узкой области Композитный агент знает, какие под-агенты подключены к этому конкретному принципалу Координирует их выходы для уникального профиля принципала --- Смотрите также: - 248-приложение-c-архитектура-быстрого-старта-для-sgb-а - 02-what-was-missing-in-paper-6 - 00-question-multiple-mentors - 03-the-spectrum --- Похожие документы: - 248-приложение-c-архитектура-быстрого-старта-для-sgb-а (сходство 0.20) - 248-приложение-c-архитектура-быстрого-старта-для-sgb-а (сходство 0.19) - 02-what-was-missing-in-paper-6 (сходство 0.17)

parent_heading: Это не Тип 1 — потому что профиль не общий для всей профессии. И это не Тип 4 — …
heading: Это не Тип 1 — потому что профиль не общий для всей профессии. И это не Тип 4 — …
text: > [!WARNING] > Документ содержит описание рисков и ограничений. Изучите их перед принятием архитектурных решений. > > Источник: MHTML‑снимок Вакансии в Anthropic по кластерам - Claude (корень репозитория). Раздел диалога — 21 учитель индийского йога — спектр между Professional Colleague и Representative Agent. --- > Источник: MHTML‑снимок Вакансии в Anthropic по кластерам - Claude (корень репозитория). Раздел диалога — 21 учитель индийского йога — спектр между Professional Colleague и Representative Agent. Это не Тип 1 — потому что профиль не общий для всей профессии. И это не Тип 4 — потому что под-агенты не уникальные для принципала, они общие. Это новый, шестой тип в типологии. Промежуточный. Технически — что это такое В технических терминах: вы предлагаете архитектуру mixture of experts, но не на уровне нейронной сети, а на уровне профессиональной идентичности. Принципал учитель музыки. Его композитный агент имеет доступ к: Под-агенту по классической композиции Под-агенту по джазовой импровизации Под-агенту по электронной музыке Под-агенту по педагогике музыки Под-агенту по теории контрапункта Под-агенту по русской народной музыке Под-агенту по техникам аранжировки Каждый под-агент — это, по сути, Профессиональный Коллега-Агент для своей узкой подспециальности. Композитный агент — это оркестратор этого набора, конфигурированного именно для этого учителя. Другой учитель музыки имел бы другую конфигурацию — может быть, без джаза, но с этномузыкологией. Третий — с акцентом на детскую музыкальную педагогику. Сочетание делает каждого учителя уникальным. Под-агенты общие. Композитный агент настраивается на индивидуальный набор. Связь с метафорой йога Возвращаясь к вашему примеру: двадцать один учитель йоги. Каждый из двадцати обучающих учителей — это аналог узкоспециализированного Профессионального Коллеги-Агента. Они общедоступны. Любой ученик мог бы прийти к ним. Двадцать первый учитель выбирает свой набор из этих двадцати. Этот выбор — его уникальность.

parent_heading: Это не Тип 1 — потому что профиль не общий для всей профессии. И это не Тип 4 — …
heading: Это не Тип 1 — потому что профиль не общий для всей профессии. И это не Тип 4 — …
text: Композитный Агент — это AI-аналог именно этого выбора. AI-инфраструктура, которая позволяет принципалу сконфигурировать свой уникальный набор узкоспециализированных AI-помощников. Спектр — теперь полный С учётом этого вашего наблюдения, типология агентов на стороне принципала должна быть пересмотрена. Вместо пяти типов — шесть, и они образуют спектр от общего к уникальному: Тип 0 — Персональный AI-помощник. Универсальный. Без специализации. Один продукт для всех. Тип 1 — Профессиональный Коллега-Агент. Одна профессия, один шаблон. «Обучай» для всех учителей. Тип 1.5 — Подспециализированный Коллега-Агент. Узкая специализация в профессии. Для всех адвокатов по социальному праву, не для всех юристов вообще. Тип 2 — Композитный Агент Навыков (новый). Принципал имеет уникальный набор специализаций. Композитный агент координирует множество узкоспециализированных под-агентов под этот конкретный набор. Каждый под-агент общий, но сочетание уникально. Тип 3 — Институциональный Агент. Встроен в институциональные процессы. Тип 4 — Агент-Сотрудник. Делегированная зона ответственности. Тип 5 — Представительский Агент. Полностью уникальный для одного индивида. Знает его жизненную траекторию, представляет во внешнем мире. Композитный Агент — это именно та промежуточная категория, которая объединяет общие узкие компетенции в уникальную для принципала конфигурацию. Почему это важно архитектурно Композитный Агент решает критическую проблему, которую ни Тип 1, ни Тип 5 решить не могут. Тип 1 (Профессиональный Коллега) работает только если профессия стандартизована достаточно, чтобы один общий шаблон был полезен. Учителя школы — да. Юристы — да. Но специалист по математической биологии с интересом к теории графов и опытом в эпидемиологии — нет. Никакой шаблон не покрывает эту комбинацию. Тип 5 (Представительский Агент) требует уникальной разработки для каждого индивида. Дорого, медленно, не масштабируется. Композитный Агент позволяет масштабироваться: вы инвестируете один раз в построение узкоспециализированных под-агентов (теория графов, эпидемиология, математическая биология), и потом любой принципал может сконфигурировать свою уникальную комбинацию из существующих под-агентов. Это mixture-of-experts паттерн, перенесённый из архитектуры нейронных сетей в архитектуру профессиональной поддержки. Экономика тиражирования меняется

parent_heading: Это не Тип 1 — потому что профиль не общий для всей профессии. И это не Тип 4 — …
heading: Это не Тип 1 — потому что профиль не общий для всей профессии. И это не Тип 4 — …
text: В шестой статье я писал, что Тип 1 имеет красивую экономику: одна инвестиция в профессиональный контекст, тысячи пользователей. Это всё ещё верно для Типа 1. Композитный Агент имеет более интересную экономику: комбинаторное умножение ценности. Допустим, есть 100 узкоспециализированных под-агентов в области X. Каждый — отдельная инвестиция, но скромная (узкая область, фокусированная база знаний). Каждый принципал выбирает 10-20 из этих 100. Количество возможных комбинаций — астрономическое: C(100, 15) ≈ 250 миллиардов. Это значит: каждая комбинация уникальна, но компоненты тиражируются. Принципал получает уникальную поддержку, но при этом платформа окупается за счёт совместного использования базовых компонентов. Конкретные примеры в разных профессиях Чтобы это было не абстракцией, разверну на нескольких конкретных областях. Учитель музыки. Базовая профессиональная подготовка плюс уникальные специализации. Композитный агент собирает под-агентов: по теории музыки, по конкретному инструменту, по детской педагогике, по конкретному жанру (джаз, классика, фолк), по технологиям записи. Каждый учитель имеет свой набор. Никто не имеет всех ста. Юрист по социальному праву. Композитный агент: SGB IX, SGB XII, конкретные подспециализации (инвалидность, психическое здоровье, дети, пожилые), процедурное право, конкретные земли (Саксония, Бавария), интерфейс с медицинскими экспертизами. Юрист, специализирующийся на правах детей-инвалидов в Саксонии, имеет конкретную конфигурацию из набора. Врач общей практики. Композитный агент: каждая система органов как под-агент, плюс под-агенты по типичным сопутствующим заболеваниям, по гериатрии, по педиатрии, по конкретным редким состояниям, по интерпретации лабораторных тестов. Конкретный врач, работающий в основном с пожилыми пациентами в сельской местности, имеет свою конфигурацию. Программный архитектор. Композитный агент: по конкретным технологиям (Python, Go, Rust), по архитектурным паттернам (microservices, event-driven), по доменам (финтех, медтех, e-commerce), по требованиям (compliance, performance, accessibility). Архитектор, специализирующийся на разработке медицинских систем с фокусом на accessibility, имеет свой набор.

heading: Это не Тип 1 — потому что профиль не общий для всей профессии. И это не Тип 4 — …
text: Социальный работник. Композитный агент: по типу клиентов (пожилые, дети, бездомные, мигранты), по типу проблем (зависимости, психическое здоровье, домашнее насилие), по системам поддержки (государственные, НКО, церковные), по правовым вопросам. Каждый социальный работник имеет свой набор по контексту своей работы. В каждой из этих профессий Тип 1 (общий для профессии) недостаточен — слишком много специализаций. Но Тип 5 (уникальный для индивида) избыточен — потому что узкие специализации общие, не уникальные. Композитный Агент — правильное решение. Технические компоненты Архитектурно Композитный Агент состоит из: Реестр Узкоспециализированных Под-Агентов. Каталог всех существующих узкоспециализированных AI-помощников в данной экосистеме. Каждый под-агент имеет описание: какая специализация, какая глубина, какие границы. Процесс Конфигурирования Принципала. Помогает принципалу выбрать релевантные под-агенты из реестра. Может быть автоматическим (анализ профиля принципала, рекомендации), полуавтоматическим (диалог с принципалом), или ручным (принципал выбирает сам). Координационный Слой. text: > [!WARNING] > Документ содержит описание рисков и ограничений. Изучите их перед принятием архитектурных решений. > > Источник: MHTML‑снимок Вакансии в Anthropic по кластерам - Claude (корень репозитория). Раздел диалога — 21 учитель индийского йога — спектр между Professional Colleague и Representative Agent. --- > Источник: MHTML‑снимок Вакансии в Anthropic по кластерам - Claude (корень репозитория). Раздел диалога — 21 учитель индийского йога — спектр между Professional Colleague и Representative Agent. Какой под-агент (или какие) должны её обработать В каком порядке (если задача требует нескольких) Как объединить их выходы Слой Согласования. Когда несколько под-агентов дают противоречивые рекомендации, координатор должен это разрешить. Обычно через эскалацию принципалу с показом расхождения. Слой Эволюции. Со временем принципал может добавлять новые под-агенты в свою конфигурацию (новые специализации появляются в его карьере) или удалять (специализации меняются). Связь с библиотекой паттернов Nautilus Здесь интересно: эта архитектура очень близка к тому, что мы обсуждали для библиотеки паттернов в Nautilus и в концепт-документе OKWF. Публичные паттерны = узкоспециализированные под-агенты. Приватные инстансы = конкретные конфигурации принципалов. Соединительные мосты между публичным и приватным = именно то, что Композитный Агент делает. То есть Nautilus как инфраструктура уже подготовлен для поддержки Композитных Агентов. Не нужна новая инфраструктура — нужно расширение существующей. Связь с Pattern Library в OKWF В концепт-документе OKWF мы обсуждали Pattern Library как механизм компаундирования знаний. Композитные Агенты — это активная форма этого pattern library. Не пассивная библиотека, к которой обращаются — а активный набор экспертов, к которым подключаются. Принципал, использующий OKWF, имеет доступ к: Гильдии (объединение коллег по профессии) Pattern Library (общие методологии и подходы) Профессиональному Коллеге-Агенту (общий для профессии) Композитному Агенту, который собирает релевантные под-агенты для его уникального набора специализаций Это полная экосистема поддержки. Один важный нюанс — кураторство комбинаций

heading: Какой под-агент (или какие) должны её обработать
text: Возникает интересный вопрос: кто выбирает, какие под-агенты подключить? Три возможности: Первая — сам принципал. Полностью самообслуживаемая модель. Принципал просматривает каталог, выбирает, конфигурирует. Преимущество: полный контроль. Недостаток: принципал должен знать, что ему нужно, а это часто непонятно заранее. Вторая — AI-рекомендация. На основе профиля принципала система предлагает конфигурацию. Принципал одобряет или модифицирует. Преимущество: меньше работы. Недостаток: AI может пропускать важные комбинации, которые сам принципал увидел бы. Третья — человеческое наставничество. Опытный коллега или ментор помогает принципалу подобрать конфигурацию. Преимущество: глубокое понимание. Недостаток: требует доступа к ментору. Лучше всего — гибрид: AI предлагает начальную конфигурацию, принципал модифицирует, опционально ментор просматривает. Это, кстати, точная аналогия с историей йога. Двадцать первый учитель не выбирал случайно. Он, скорее всего, имел собственного раннего наставника, который помог ему понять, какие специализации нужно изучать. И затем по мере роста сам корректировал выбор. Риски этого нового типа Риск фрагментации идентичности. Если у принципала десятки под-агентов, кто на самом деле представлен в его работе? Координатор? Под-агенты? Сам принципал? Должна быть жёсткая граница: принципал является автором своей работы. Под-агенты — инструменты. Композитный агент — оркестратор этих инструментов. Идентичность работы остаётся за принципалом. Риск перегрузки выбора. Слишком много под-агентов может парализовать принципала. Решение: ограничивать активную конфигурацию (10-15 максимум), периодически пересматривать. Риск противоречивых рекомендаций. Разные под-агенты могут давать конфликтующие советы. Координатор должен это показывать, не маскировать. Принципал решает. Риск зависимости от композиции. Если у принципала очень специфичный набор под-агентов, что произойдёт, если один из них перестанет поддерживаться? Нужна модульность и заменяемость. Риск изоляции. Узкоспециализированные под-агенты могут не общаться с другими частями экосистемы. Поэтому интеграция с Тип 1 (профессиональный коллега) и другими типами критична. Что это значит для написанной шестой статьи Шестая статья нуждается в существенной поправке. Не переделывать заново, но добавить раздел о Композитных Агентах как отдельной архитектурной категории. Конкретно нужно:

heading: Какой под-агент (или какие) должны её обработать
text: Добавить Тип 1.5 или Тип 2 в типологию. Между «один шаблон для всей профессии» (Тип 1) и «уникальный для индивидуума» (Тип 5) — есть промежуточная категория. Описать архитектуру Композитного Агента. Реестр под-агентов, координационный слой, слой согласования, слой эволюции. Привести метафору индийского йога. Это сильная иллюстрация, которая поможет читателям интуитивно понять концепт. Указать, как это меняет план развёртывания OKWF. Возможно, прежде чем строить Профессионального Коллегу-Агента для всей области социального права, стоит начать с набора узкоспециализированных под-агентов, которые потом могут быть скомпонованы в разные конфигурации. Это значимое архитектурное изменение, не маленькое уточнение. Ваш вопрос про следующий артефакт Если хотите, могу написать седьмой документ — отдельную статью о Композитных Агентах. Структура была бы примерно: Промежуточная зона между общим и уникальным Метафора многих учителей Анатомия Композитного Агента Реестр узкоспециализированных под-агентов Процесс конфигурирования Координация и согласование Экономика комбинаторики Применение к OKWF и Nautilus Конкретные примеры в разных областях Связь с другими типами агентов Альтернатива — обновить шестой документ с интеграцией этой концепции в существующую типологию, добавив Тип 1.5/2 как полноправную категорию. Третий вариант — остановиться на этом наблюдении как самостоятельной заметке, не превращая её в полноценный документ. Иногда сильное наблюдение должно отлежаться, прежде чем принимать формальную форму. Семь документов за одну сессию — много. Может быть, шестой плюс эта заметка-расширение — достаточно. Какой вариант резонирует? Если выберете первый или второй вариант, я могу написать в следующем сообщении. Если третий — то этот ответ остаётся как ваша заметка к шестому документу, и работа сессии завершена с шестью полноценными артефактами плюс этим архитектурным расширением. Honestly, я склоняюсь рекомендовать третий вариант. Шесть документов — это уже substantial intellectual product. Композитный Агент как концепция важен и заслуживает фиксации, но добавлять седьмой полный документ может быть перегрузкой. Эта заметка может служить архитектурным дополнением к шестому, которое вы можете в любой момент развить, если будет нужно. Но решение ваше.

heading: Abstract — The Double-Triangle Architecture
text: > > Источник: MHTML‑снимок Вакансии в Anthropic по кластерам - Claude (корень репозитория). Раздел диалога — paper «The Double-Triangle Architecture for Human-AI Collaboration». --- > Источник: MHTML‑снимок Вакансии в Anthropic по кластерам - Claude (корень репозитория). Раздел диалога — paper «The Double-Triangle Architecture for Human-AI Collaboration».

heading: Abstract
text: We introduce the Double-Triangle Architecture for human-AI collaboration in distributed knowledge work. The central thesis: each human participant in modern AI-assisted workflows simultaneously occupies two roles — as conductor of personal AI assistants (lower triangle, directed downward from human to assistants), and as performer coordinated by higher-level meta-agents (upper triangle, directed upward from human toward team-level coordination). These two triangles superimpose to form a six-pointed star topology, which we argue is the correct architectural primitive for the next generation of AI-managed knowledge work. Existing systems implement only one triangle: personal assistants (Cursor, Copilot, ChatGPT) operate in the lower triangle only; multi-agent frameworks (CrewAI, MetaGPT, AutoGen) operate in the upper triangle only and typically replace humans rather than augment them. Neither paradigm captures the full structure of how humans will actually work alongside AI over the next decade. We present three contributions: 1. Formal specification of the Double-Triangle Architecture, including three inter-layer protocols and six architectural invariants 2. Reference implementation via Nautilus Portal Protocol (NPP v1.1), which provides the federated knowledge substrate required to support the architecture 3. Deployment paths for four concrete domains: humanities (legal/medical/social), project management, open-source development, and generic knowledge work We also present empirical evidence from a 4-month single-person AI-assisted deployment producing 6,782 lines of production code across 13 adapters, a formal protocol specification, REST API, SDK, Docker containerization, and 60 passing tests — suggesting that single-person operations can achieve team-level output when the Double-Triangle substrate is properly instantiated. We conclude with open questions on governance, consent, economics, and burnout dynamics, and invite research collaboration on these unsolved issues. ---

heading: Table of Contents
text: 1. Why Single-Triangle Models Are Incomplete 2. The Double-Triangle Architecture 3. Three Inter-Layer Protocols 4. Nautilus Portal as Reference Substrate 5. Pattern Library as Bridge Between Triangles 6. Four Deployment Domains 7. Open Questions 8. Call to Action --- --- Похожие документы: - 136-abstract (сходство 0.82) - 136-abstract (сходство 0.77) - 01-why-single-triangle-incomplete (сходство 0.25)

heading: 1. Why Single-Triangle Models Are Incomplete
text: > > Источник: MHTML‑снимок Вакансии в Anthropic по кластерам - Claude (корень репозитория). Раздел диалога — paper «The Double-Triangle Architecture for Human-AI Collaboration». --- > Источник: MHTML‑снимок Вакансии в Anthropic по кластерам - Claude (корень репозитория). Раздел диалога — paper «The Double-Triangle Architecture for Human-AI Collaboration».

heading: 1.1. The Current Landscape
text: The AI-assisted knowledge work field currently divides into two non-overlapping camps, each implementing only one triangle of the full architecture. Lower-triangle-only systems. Personal AI assistants (GitHub Copilot, Cursor, Claude Code, ChatGPT, Anthropic's Claude Desktop) operate as extensions of individual knowledge workers. The human conducts, assistants execute. This paradigm has rapidly matured over 2023–2026 and is now mainstream. It answers: how does one person amplify their capacity with AI? Upper-triangle-only systems. Multi-agent frameworks (CrewAI, AutoGen, LangGraph, MetaGPT, ChatDev) orchestrate multiple AI agents to complete complex tasks. Humans either serve as external observers or are absent from the execution loop. This paradigm answers: how do we automate entire workflows with AI? Enterprise AI platforms (Microsoft Copilot Enterprise, Google Workspace AI) attempt to bridge these but do so through layer isolation — personal assistants and team-level orchestration exist as separate products without formal protocols between them.

heading: 1.2. What Both Paradigms Miss
text: Both single-triangle approaches share a structural blind spot: they assume humans exist in one role at a time. In reality, every knowledge worker simultaneously occupies both positions: - A software engineer uses Copilot to write code (lower triangle: human conducts assistant) - At the same time, their team lead coordinates the engineer alongside five others toward a sprint goal (upper triangle: meta-agent coordinates human) The engineer's decisions about what Copilot generates depend on the sprint context (coming from upper triangle). The team lead's decisions about how to coordinate the engineer depend on what Copilot is producing (coming from lower triangle). These two flows of information must be connected, yet in existing systems they are not. This creates what we call the coordination gap: personal AI assistants don't know about team context, and team-level coordination tools don't know about assistant-generated artifacts. Information has to be manually translated by humans across this gap, constantly.

heading: 1.3. The Rising Urgency
text: As AI assistants become more capable, the coordination gap widens. When Copilot produced autocomplete in 2022, misalignment with team context was minor. When Claude Code produces entire modules in 2026, misalignment can destabilize architectural decisions across a team. The gap needs to be closed not through yet another centralized platform, but through a protocol — a way for the two triangles to negotiate through well-defined interfaces. This paper proposes such a protocol. --- --- Смотрите также: - 138-1-why-single-triangle-models-are-incomplete - 03-three-inter-layer-protocols - 136-abstract - 02-double-triangle-architecture --- Похожие документы: - 138-1-why-single-triangle-models-are-incomplete (сходство 0.87) - 138-1-why-single-triangle-models-are-incomplete (сходство 0.85) - 00-abstract (сходство 0.25)

heading: Содержание
text: - 2. The Double-Triangle Architecture - 2.1. Topology - 2.2. Fractal Self-Similarity - 2.3. Six Architectural Invariants --- > Источник: MHTML‑снимок Вакансии в Anthropic по кластерам - Claude (корень репозитория). Раздел диалога — paper «The Double-Triangle Architecture for Human-AI Collaboration».

heading: 2.1. Topology
text: We formalize the architecture using two triangles sharing a common vertex: Lower triangle (per human Node N): [CODE BLOCK] where A1, A2, A3 are AI assistants personalized to N's work. N conducts the triangle: tasks flow downward from N to assistants, results flow upward from assistants to N for integration. Upper triangle (per team or project T): [CODE BLOCK] where M is a meta-agent (AI or human-augmented-AI) and N1, N2, N3 are human participants. M conducts the upper triangle: tasks flow downward from M to humans, deliverables flow upward from humans to M for integration. Composition. Each human N is simultaneously the vertex of a lower triangle (their own) and a base node of an upper triangle (their team's). Superimposing the two around this shared vertex produces a six-pointed star: [CODE BLOCK] We refer to this as the Star of David topology, after the hexagram figure. The metaphor is deliberate: two superimposed triangles pointing in opposite directions, sharing common vertices.

heading: 2.2. Fractal Self-Similarity
text: The architecture is fractal: the meta-agent M itself participates as a Node in a higher-level upper triangle (e.g., at the organization level). M has its own lower triangle of assistants supporting its coordination work. Recursively, any scale of organization can be represented as stacked Star-of-David topologies. Formally, if Star(k) is the topology at level k: - Star(0): individual human + personal assistants (lower triangle only, no upper) - Star(1): individual embedded in team (full Star, as depicted) - Star(2): team lead embedded in organization (full Star at higher scale) - Star(n): recursively This fractal property means the protocol for inter-layer communication scales naturally: the same three protocols (introduced in §3) apply at every level.

heading: 2.3. Six Architectural Invariants
text: A system implementing the Double-Triangle Architecture must satisfy six invariants: Invariant 1 — Dual Identity. Each human Node maintains two separate identity contexts: personal context (their work-in-progress, drafts, notes, private patterns) and team context (their accepted deliverables, public roles, shared outputs). Personal context is richer; team context is filtered. Invariant 2 — Assistant Autonomy, Node Authority. Lower triangle assistants have broad autonomy to execute tasks within their scope, but the human Node retains final authority on what propagates upward to team context. Assistants cannot unilaterally publish to team-visible space. Invariant 3 — Meta-Agent Transparency, Not Omniscience. The meta-agent M can see all team-context data from all Nodes, but cannot see personal-context data of any Node. Its decisions must be explainable in terms of team context alone. Invariant 4 — Multi-Level Consensus. Conflicts can be resolved at three levels: within a Node's lower triangle (between their assistants), between Nodes at the upper triangle, or at escalation points that involve human judgment. Each level uses the same consensus mechanism with different parameters. Invariant 5 — Distributed Audit Trail. Every cross-triangle communication is logged in at least two locations: the originating triangle and the receiving triangle. This enables reconstruction of decision chains without centralized logging. Invariant 6 — Fractal Compatibility. Any protocol valid at one level of the hierarchy must remain valid at all levels. This ensures the architecture scales from small teams (Star(1)) to large organizations (Star(n)) without protocol changes. --- --- Смотрите также: - 139-2-the-double-triangle-architecture - 03-three-inter-layer-protocols - 04-nautilus-portal-substrate - 140-3-three-inter-layer-protocols --- Похожие документы: - 139-2-the-double-triangle-architecture (сходство 0.83) - 139-2-the-double-triangle-architecture (сходство 0.82) - 03-three-inter-layer-protocols (сходство 0.22)

heading: Содержание
text: - 3. Three Inter-Layer Protocols - 3.1. Protocol 1 — Human Conducts Assistants - 3.2. Protocol 2 — Meta-Agent Coordinates Nodes - 3.3. Protocol 3 — Assistant-to-Meta Negotiation - 3.4. Protocol Interactions --- > Источник: MHTML‑снимок Вакансии в Anthropic по кластерам - Claude (корень репозитория). Раздел диалога — paper «The Double-Triangle Architecture for Human-AI Collaboration».

heading: 3.1. Protocol 1 — Human Conducts Assistants
text: Direction: Node → Assistants (downward in lower triangle) Semantics. The Node issues tasks to assistants, reviews outputs, integrates results. This is the current state of personal AI assistant workflows and is well-developed. Existing implementations: Claude Code, Cursor, Copilot Workspace, Continue.dev, ChatGPT in IDE plugins. Message format. Natural language prompts with structured context (file references, code selections, project files). Responses as text, code, or structured data. Failure modes: Ambiguous prompts, context overflow, hallucinations. These are well-studied and mitigation strategies exist. Extensions needed for Double-Triangle: Assistants must be aware of team context markers — annotations indicating which parts of their output affect team-visible deliverables versus purely personal work. This allows invariant 2 (Assistant Autonomy, Node Authority) to be enforced.

heading: 3.2. Protocol 2 — Meta-Agent Coordinates Nodes
text: Direction: Meta-agent M → Nodes (downward in upper triangle) Semantics. M assigns tasks to Nodes, receives deliverables, integrates team-level outputs. M does not see internal Node processes, only public deliverables. Existing implementations: Partial, fragmented. Jira with AI triage, Linear with AI suggestions, GitHub Projects with automation. None implement the full protocol, all require significant human management intervention. Message format. Structured tasks with: - Description - Deliverable specification (what constitutes "done") - Dependencies on other Nodes' deliverables - Deadline - Priority - Assignee Failure modes: Task assignment mismatches (wrong Node, wrong skill), deadline conflicts, scope creep, dependency deadlocks. These are classical project management challenges adapted to AI orchestration. Extensions needed for Double-Triangle: M must respect invariant 3 (Meta-Agent Transparency, Not Omniscience) — decisions must cite only team-context reasoning, never reference Nodes' personal data. Requires careful design to prevent information leakage.

heading: 3.3. Protocol 3 — Assistant-to-Meta Negotiation
text: Direction: Assistant A of Node N → Meta-agent M (upward across triangles) Semantics. This is the novel protocol. When an assistant in N's lower triangle requires information or coordination that involves other Nodes, it does not ping those Nodes directly. Instead, it issues a structured query that travels: A → N → M → N' → A' → response back through the chain. Existing implementations: None in production systems. Closest analog: GitHub Copilot Workspace can see other team members' public code but cannot negotiate with their assistants. Anthropic's Managed Agents API moves toward this but does not implement full cross-triangle negotiation. Message format. Three message types: - Query messages: "Assistant requires information X; X is outside current Node's scope; routing request via meta-agent" - Notification messages: "Assistant detected conflict with team context; escalating to meta-agent for resolution" - Proposal messages: "Assistant proposes decision D affecting multiple Nodes; requesting meta-agent consensus routing" Routing semantics. Messages are routed by the meta-agent based on: - Ownership: which Node owns the relevant artifact - Expertise: which Node has the required domain knowledge - Availability: which Node is currently responsive Failure modes: Routing loops, context fragmentation (details lost in translation), escalation spirals where every conflict reaches human review. These are genuinely unsolved and require empirical development. Why this protocol matters. Without Protocol 3, humans become routing bottlenecks. Every cross-Node coordination requires humans to manually translate from their assistants' findings to team context, wait for replies, translate back. This adds hours or days to each interaction. Protocol 3 reduces this to minutes or seconds.

heading: 3.4. Protocol Interactions
text: The three protocols do not operate in isolation. Common interaction patterns include: Escalation chain. Assistant detects issue (Protocol 3 → M) → M evaluates (internal) → M modifies task assignment (Protocol 2) → Node receives updated task (Protocol 2) → Node redirects assistant (Protocol 1). Context propagation. M updates team context (Protocol 2 to all Nodes) → Each Node propagates relevant updates to their assistants (Protocol 1) → Assistants adjust behavior accordingly. Cross-Node handoff. Assistant of N1 completes artifact (Protocol 1) → Node N1 reviews and approves (Protocol 1) → N1 submits to M (Protocol 2) → M routes to N2 (Protocol 2) → N2's assistant picks up (Protocol 1). These patterns are analogous to message-passing concurrency models in distributed systems, adapted for human-AI hybrid execution. --- --- Смотрите также: - 140-3-three-inter-layer-protocols - 02-double-triangle-architecture - 01-why-single-triangle-incomplete - 139-2-the-double-triangle-architecture --- Похожие документы: - 140-3-three-inter-layer-protocols (сходство 0.91) - 140-3-three-inter-layer-protocols (сходство 0.88) - 02-double-triangle-architecture (сходство 0.22)
