heading: 2. Terminology
text: > > Источник: MHTML‑снимок Вакансии в Anthropic по кластерам - Claude (корень репозитория). Раздел диалога — Nautilus Portal Protocol v1.1 RFC, написанный совместно с Claude. --- > Источник: MHTML‑снимок Вакансии в Anthropic по кластерам - Claude (корень репозитория). Раздел диалога — Nautilus Portal Protocol v1.1 RFC, написанный совместно с Claude.

heading: 2. Terminology
text: Ecosystem — набор репозиториев, участвующих в одной федерации. Имеет уникальное имя (например, svend4). Repository-participant (далее — Repo) — Git-репозиторий, содержащий минимум nautilus.json в корне и/или зарегистрированный в registry портала. Native format — исходный формат данных в Repo, определяемый автором. Может быть любым: Markdown, JSON, YAML, plain text, бинарный формат. NPP не ограничивает native format. Portal — implementation NPP, которая загружает registry, инстанцирует адаптеры и выполняет query-операции. Reference implementation: github.com/svend4/nautilus, но альтернативные порталы совместимы через соответствие этому протоколу. Registry — файл nautilus.json в корне Portal-репо, перечисляющий все Repos экосистемы с их метаданными. Adapter — Python-модуль (или эквивалент на другом языке), реализующий BaseAdapter interface и переводящий native format Repo в унифицированный PortalEntry. Passport — passport.md файл, human-readable описание Repo, располагается в passports/<format>.md в Portal-репо. Валидируется по passportschema.json. PortalEntry — унифицированная структура данных, в которую адаптеры конвертируют native-записи при fetch. Bridge — декларативное описание моста между концепциями разных Repos. Описан в nautilus.json в поле bridges и в passport соответствующего репо. Consensus — результат сопоставления найденных концептов между несколькими Repos. Может быть full (coverage = 1.0), partial (coverage < 1.0), absent (coverage = 0). Compatibility Level — целое число от 0 до 3, характеризующее степень интеграции Repo с экосистемой. Определение в разделе 5. Q6-координата — 6-битная строка (строка символов «0» и «1» длиной 6), обозначающая позицию концепта в 6-мерном бинарном гиперкубе {0,1}⁶ (64 вершины). Q6-соседство — отношение между Q6-координатами с расстоянием Хэмминга ≤ N. По умолчанию N = 1 (соседи, отличающиеся в одном бите). Fallback — адаптер вернул статические (заранее заготовленные) данные, а не результат реального поиска. Обозначается PortalEntry.isfallback = True. Protocol Version — semver-версия NPP, объявляемая в nautilus.json поле protocolversion (или nautilusversion для v1.x из-за исторических причин). Onboarding Path — один из пяти стандартных способов подключения Repo к экосистеме (A–E, см. раздел 12). --- ---

heading: 3. Registry (`nautilus.json`)
text: > [!IMPORTANT] > Ключевой документ для понимания архитектуры. Рекомендуется прочитать в первую очередь. > > Источник: MHTML‑снимок Вакансии в Anthropic по кластерам - Claude (корень репозитория). Раздел диалога — Nautilus Portal Protocol v1.1 RFC, написанный совместно с Claude. --- > Источник: MHTML‑снимок Вакансии в Anthropic по кластерам - Claude (корень репозитория). Раздел диалога — Nautilus Portal Protocol v1.1 RFC, написанный совместно с Claude.

heading: 3.3. Required Fields
text: - protocolversion — строка в формате semver ("1.0", "1.1"). Портал MUST отклонять registry с protocolversion, не поддерживаемым этим порталом - ecosystemname — короткое уникальное имя (latin, без пробелов) - registry — массив. MUST содержать минимум один элемент Каждый элемент registry MUST содержать: - name — уникальное в пределах экосистемы - format — идентификатор native-формата (используется для именования адаптера и passport) - adapter — имя адаптера: либо имя Python-модуля в adapters/, либо "auto" для использования AutoAdapter (см. раздел 12.3) - compatibility — целое 0..3

heading: 3.4. Optional Fields
text: - repo, url — если опущены, Repo существует только локально - nativeunit — human-readable описание единицы данных - passport — путь к файлу в формате passports/<format>.md. Если опущен, адаптер MUST всё равно быть functional - angle — semantic/methodological/symbolic/other — указывает "угол зрения" Repo на общие концепты - q6key — правило проекции native-сущностей в Q6 (например, "hexid - 1 → bin(6)" для meta) - bridges — объект: ключи — имена других Repos, значения — текстовые описания семантических мостов

heading: 3.5. Validation Rules
text: Portal MUST отклонять registry, если: - JSON невалиден - Missing required fields в любом элементе registry - compatibility вне диапазона 0..3 - Дубликаты name в пределах одной экосистемы - protocolversion не поддерживается порталом Portal SHOULD выдавать warning (но не отклонять), если: - repo или url указаны, но недоступны - adapter указан, но модуль не найден - bridges ссылаются на name, которых нет в registry - passport указан, но файл не найден

heading: 3.6. AutoAdapter Special Case
text: Если adapter: "auto", portal MUST использовать AutoAdapter (см. раздел 12.3), который загружает nautilus.json из корня target-репо вместо локального модуля-адаптера. Это enables Onboarding Path C (self-declaring repo). --- --- Смотрите также: - 78-3-registry-nautilus-json - 08-3-registry-nautilus-json - 07-portal-entry - 02-terminology --- Похожие документы: - 78-3-registry-nautilus-json (сходство 0.84) - 78-3-registry-nautilus-json (сходство 0.82) - 03-registry (сходство 0.54)

heading: 4. Passport (`passport.md`)
text: > > Источник: MHTML‑снимок Вакансии в Anthropic по кластерам - Claude (корень репозитория). Раздел диалога — Nautilus Portal Protocol v1.1 RFC, написанный совместно с Claude. --- > Источник: MHTML‑снимок Вакансии в Anthropic по кластерам - Claude (корень репозитория). Раздел диалога — Nautilus Portal Protocol v1.1 RFC, написанный совместно с Claude.

heading: 4.1. Purpose
text: Passport — human-readable описание Repo, расположенное в passports/<format>.md в Portal-репо (в отличие от v1.0, где Passport жил в самом Repo). Passport предназначен для людей, читающих Repo впервые, для адаптеров, которые могут его парсить для discovery, и для автоматических систем валидации (через JSON-Schema).

heading: 4.3. Recommended Sections
text: Passport SHOULD содержать разделы: - Описание — 2-3 предложения о содержании Repo - Объём — количество единиц, связей - Q6-отображение — правило проекции на 6-битный гиперкуб - Мосты — таблица bridges к другим Repos - Примеры запросов — 3-5 query-string, дающих хорошие результаты - Доступ к данным — static githubapi httpapi localfiles + требования к токенам

heading: 4.5. Naming Convention
text: - Passport-файлы именуются passports/<format>.md, не passports/<repo-name>.md - Причина: один format может обслуживаться несколькими репо (например, domain-sub-adapters в рамках pro2), но passport — один на format --- --- Смотрите также: - 79-4-passport-passport-md - 09-4-passport-passport-md - 03-registry - 16-mcp-extension --- Похожие документы: - 79-4-passport-passport-md (сходство 0.76) - 79-4-passport-passport-md (сходство 0.74) - 04-passport (сходство 0.26)

heading: 5. Compatibility Levels
text: > > Источник: MHTML‑снимок Вакансии в Anthropic по кластерам - Claude (корень репозитория). Раздел диалога — Nautilus Portal Protocol v1.1 RFC, написанный совместно с Claude. --- > Источник: MHTML‑снимок Вакансии в Anthropic по кластерам - Claude (корень репозитория). Раздел диалога — Nautilus Portal Protocol v1.1 RFC, написанный совместно с Claude.

heading: 5. Compatibility Levels
text: NPP определяет 4 уровня совместимости Repo с экосистемой. Это позволяет постепенное подключение. Repo объявлен в nautilus.json. Адаптера нет. Portal знает о существовании Repo, но не может запрашивать. Use case: анонс намерения подключить Repo до написания кода. Требования: - Только запись в nautilus.json (минимально name + format + repo)

heading: Level 1 — Readable
text: Repo имеет passport + адаптер со статическими записями. Portal возвращает static entries. Use case: архивы, reference docs, стабильные репо. Требования: - Всё из Level 0 - passport.md в passports/ - Адаптер реализует fetch() + describe() (см. раздел 6) - Fallback-entries в адаптере (≥3 записи)

heading: Level 2 — Linked
text: Адаптер производит записи с заполненными Q6-координатами в metadata.q6 и links на другие Repos. Portal может вычислять кросс-ссылки и визуализировать граф. Use case: активные Repos с семантическими связями. Требования: - Всё из Level 1 - Каждый PortalEntry MUST иметь metadata.q6 (если Q6-маппинг определён для этого Repo) - Каждый PortalEntry SHOULD иметь ≥1 link на другой Repo

heading: Level 3 — Interactive
text: Адаптер выполняет live-fetch через GitHub API или эквивалент, возвращая реальные данные из Repo (не только fallback). Use case: Repos, глубоко интегрированные с экосистемой. Требования: - Всё из Level 2 - fetch() делает real network call (с timeout ≤ 5 сек) - Graceful fallback: при ошибке сети/API возвращает static entries с isfallback=True - PortalEntry.isfallback явно помечается --- --- Смотрите также: - 80-5-compatibility-levels - 17-5-compatibility-levels - 07-portal-entry - 06-adapter-interface --- Похожие документы: - 80-5-compatibility-levels (сходство 0.80) - 80-5-compatibility-levels (сходство 0.72) - 05-compatibility-levels (сходство 0.36)

heading: 6. Adapter Interface
text: > [!IMPORTANT] > Ключевой документ для понимания архитектуры. Рекомендуется прочитать в первую очередь. > > Источник: MHTML‑снимок Вакансии в Anthropic по кластерам - Claude (корень репозитория). Раздел диалога — Nautilus Portal Protocol v1.1 RFC, написанный совместно с Claude. --- > Источник: MHTML‑снимок Вакансии в Anthropic по кластерам - Claude (корень репозитория). Раздел диалога — Nautilus Portal Protocol v1.1 RFC, написанный совместно с Claude.

heading: 6.2. `fetch(query)` — Required
text: Accepts string query, returns list of PortalEntry. Implementation MUST: - Возвращать пустой список или fallback-entries, если ничего не найдено (NOT None, NOT raise exception) - Ограничить результат разумным числом (SHOULD ≤ 100) - Не выполнять сетевые запросы к другим Repos (только свой Repo или external API) - При ошибке возвращать fallback с isfallback=True Implementation SHOULD: - Поддерживать case-insensitive matching - Возвращать результаты в порядке убывания релевантности - Кешировать результаты для одинаковых query (TTL по дизайну implementation, RECOMMENDED 24 часа)

heading: 6.4. Type Safety
text: Reference implementation требует mypy-clean код. Альтернативные implementations SHOULD, но не MUST, придерживаться strict typing. --- --- Смотрите также: - 81-6-adapter-interface - 18-6-adapter-interface - 07-portal-entry - 16-mcp-extension --- Похожие документы: - 81-6-adapter-interface (сходство 0.71) - 81-6-adapter-interface (сходство 0.71) - 06-adapter-interface (сходство 0.57)

heading: 7. PortalEntry Structure
text: > [!IMPORTANT] > Ключевой документ для понимания архитектуры. Рекомендуется прочитать в первую очередь. > > Источник: MHTML‑снимок Вакансии в Anthropic по кластерам - Claude (корень репозитория). Раздел диалога — Nautilus Portal Protocol v1.1 RFC, написанный совместно с Claude. --- > Источник: MHTML‑снимок Вакансии в Anthropic по кластерам - Claude (корень репозитория). Раздел диалога — Nautilus Portal Protocol v1.1 RFC, написанный совместно с Claude.

heading: 7.1. Field Semantics
text: - id MUST быть уникален в пределах экосистемы. Формат: "<format>:<slug>" (например, "info1:alpha-3-doc-1") - title SHOULD быть до 120 символов - source — GitHub slug owner/repo-name - formattype — one of: document, concept, rule, theory, schema, archetype. Implementation MAY расширять список - content — полный текст/представление, MAY быть большим - metadata — MUST содержать q6 для Level 2+ адаптеров - links — список id из других Repos, формат "<format>:<type>:<id>" или "<format>:<id>" (например, "pro2:q6:010011", "meta:hexagram:50") - isfallback — Boolean, MUST быть True для fallback-entries, False (default) для real fetch results

heading: 7.2. Q6 Metadata
text: Для адаптеров Level 2+, каждый PortalEntry MUST содержать metadata["q6"] — 6-битную строку длиной 6, только символы "0" и "1". Пример: [CODE BLOCK] --- --- Смотрите также: - 82-7-portalentry-structure - 19-7-portalentry-structure - 06-adapter-interface - 17-versioning-policy --- Похожие документы: - 82-7-portalentry-structure (сходство 0.75) - 82-7-portalentry-structure (сходство 0.68) - 07-portal-entry (сходство 0.38)

heading: 8. Q6 Space (Normative)
text: > [!IMPORTANT] > Ключевой документ для понимания архитектуры. Рекомендуется прочитать в первую очередь. > > Источник: MHTML‑снимок Вакансии в Anthropic по кластерам - Claude (корень репозитория). Раздел диалога — Nautilus Portal Protocol v1.1 RFC, написанный совместно с Claude. --- > Источник: MHTML‑снимок Вакансии в Anthropic по кластерам - Claude (корень репозитория). Раздел диалога — Nautilus Portal Protocol v1.1 RFC, написанный совместно с Claude.

heading: 8.3. Q6 Mapping Rules
text: Каждый format определяет правило проекции своих native-сущностей в Q6. Правило MUST быть задокументировано в passport в разделе "Q6-отображение". Примеры правил (из reference implementation): Format Правило ----------------- info1 alpha + 4 → 3 старших бита, остальные биты по категории pro2 нативные Q6-координаты (Q6 — первичный концепт pro2) meta hexid - 1 → bin(6) (гексаграмма 1 → 000000, 64 → 111111) data7 порядковый номер % 64 → bin(6) Формат допускает явное указание "не определено", если Q6-маппинг не применим (Level 1 репо MAY не иметь Q6).

heading: 8.5. CA-Class Mapping (Informative)
text: Вершины Q6 MAY маппиться на классы клеточных автоматов Вольфрама (Class I–IV). Это не обязательно, но RECOMMENDED для экосистем, интегрирующих symbolic и dynamical angles: - Class I (стационарные) — стабильные концепты (базовые алгоритмы, определения) - Class II (периодические) — методология, архетипы - Class III (хаотические) — конфликт, разрушение - Class IV (сложные, edge of chaos) — синтез, emergent properties --- --- Смотрите также: - 83-8-q6-space-normative - 07-portal-entry - 20-adr-002-q6-first-class - 13-rest-api --- Похожие документы: - 83-8-q6-space-normative (сходство 0.77) - 83-8-q6-space-normative (сходство 0.76) - 07-portal-entry (сходство 0.21)

heading: 9. Consensus Algorithm
text: > > Источник: MHTML‑снимок Вакансии в Anthropic по кластерам - Claude (корень репозитория). Раздел диалога — Nautilus Portal Protocol v1.1 RFC, написанный совместно с Claude. --- > Источник: MHTML‑снимок Вакансии в Anthropic по кластерам - Claude (корень репозитория). Раздел диалога — Nautilus Portal Protocol v1.1 RFC, написанный совместно с Claude.

heading: 9.5. Future Extensions (v2.0+)
text: v2.0 MAY использовать: - Semantic similarity через embeddings (cross-lingual matching) - Weighted consensus (разный вес от confidence адаптера) - Fuzzy matching с configurable threshold Эти расширения не breaking — они будут активироваться через algorithm параметр в query, сохраняя v1.1 как default. --- --- Смотрите также: - 84-9-consensus-algorithm - 08-consensus-algorithm - 20-8-consensus-algorithm - 11-relevance-ranking --- Похожие документы: - 84-9-consensus-algorithm (сходство 0.72) - 84-9-consensus-algorithm (сходство 0.70) - 08-consensus-algorithm (сходство 0.37)

heading: 10. Query Flow
text: > > Источник: MHTML‑снимок Вакансии в Anthropic по кластерам - Claude (корень репозитория). Раздел диалога — Nautilus Portal Protocol v1.1 RFC, написанный совместно с Claude. --- > Источник: MHTML‑снимок Вакансии в Anthropic по кластерам - Claude (корень репозитория). Раздел диалога — Nautilus Portal Protocol v1.1 RFC, написанный совместно с Claude.

heading: 10.1. Lifecycle
text: 1. Client вызывает portal.query(q, targetrepos=None) 2. Portal загружает registry (cached) 3. Portal фильтрует адаптеры по targetrepos (если задан) 4. Portal параллельно вызывает adapter.fetch(q) для всех выбранных 5. Portal собирает resultsbyrepo: dict[str, list[PortalEntry]] 6. Portal вычисляет consensus по алгоритму раздела 9 7. Portal вычисляет relevance ranking (раздел 11) 8. Portal возвращает QueryResult

heading: 10.2. Parallelism
text: Adapter calls SHOULD быть параллельными (через thread pool, async, или multiprocessing). Это критично для performance при росте числа Repos. Если adapter падает (exception, timeout), Portal MUST: - Включить repo в errors список QueryResult - Продолжить обработку остальных - Не падать целиком Timeout per adapter RECOMMENDED: 5 секунд (было 10 в v1.0, снижено для лучшего UX).

heading: 10.4. Timeout Behavior
text: При timeout адаптера portal MUST: - Отметить repo как error в errors - Если у адаптера есть fallback entries — попытаться вернуть их - Логировать timeout для telemetry --- --- Смотрите также: - 85-10-query-flow - 09-query-flow - 21-9-query-flow - 11-relevance-ranking --- Похожие документы: - 85-10-query-flow (сходство 0.74) - 85-10-query-flow (сходство 0.72) - 09-query-flow (сходство 0.72)

heading: 11. Relevance Ranking
text: > > Источник: MHTML‑снимок Вакансии в Anthropic по кластерам - Claude (корень репозитория). Раздел диалога — Nautilus Portal Protocol v1.1 RFC, написанный совместно с Claude. --- > Источник: MHTML‑снимок Вакансии в Anthropic по кластерам - Claude (корень репозитория). Раздел диалога — Nautilus Portal Protocol v1.1 RFC, написанный совместно с Claude.

heading: 11.1. Scoring Formula
text: Для каждого PortalEntry e и query q (оба normalized): ```python def relevancescore(e: PortalEntry, q: str) -> float: score = 0.0 qlower = q.lower() titlelower = e.title.lower() contentlower = e.content.lower() idlower = e.id.lower() if qlower = titlelower: score += 1.0 elif qlower in titlelower: score += 0.7 if qlower in contentlower: score += 0.3 if qlower in idlower: score += 0.4

heading: 11.2. MAY Extensions
text: Альтернативные алгоритмы ранжирования (TF-IDF, BM25, semantic embeddings) могут использоваться, но MUST быть опциональными через параметр ranked=<algorithm>. Default MUST оставаться формула раздела 11.1 для воспроизводимости между порталами. --- --- Смотрите также: - 86-11-relevance-ranking - 17-appendix-b-change-log - 09-consensus-algorithm - 14-sdk --- Похожие документы: - 86-11-relevance-ranking (сходство 0.58) - 86-11-relevance-ranking (сходство 0.58) - 13-reference-implementation (сходство 0.34)

text: > > Источник: MHTML‑снимок Вакансии в Anthropic по кластерам - Claude (корень репозитория). Раздел диалога — Nautilus Portal Protocol v1.1 RFC, написанный совместно с Claude.

heading: 12. Onboarding Paths (Normative)
text: NPP v1.1 формализует пять путей подключения нового Repo как equivalent-рангованные стратегии. Каждая имеет свой trade-off между автоматизацией и качеством. Время: 10–20 минут. Автоматизация: 0%. Качество: высокое. 1. Написать adapters/<format>.py, наследуясь от BaseAdapter 2. Написать passports/<format>.md 3. Зарегистрировать адаптер в adapters/init.py 4. Добавить импорт в portal.py в конструктор 5. Добавить запись в nautilus.json

heading: 12.3. Path C — Self-Declaring Repo (AutoAdapter)
text: Время: 10 минут. Автоматизация: 80%. Качество: зависит от index в nautilus.json target-репо. 1. В целевом репо создать nautilus.json в корне с полем index 2. В portal-репо добавить запись {"adapter": "auto", "repo": "..."} 3. Portal автоматически использует AutoAdapter Ключевое свойство: repo регистрирует себя сам, portal не требует изменений кода. Это enables federation без координации.

heading: 12.4. Path D — Auto-Scanner
text: Время: 2 минуты. Автоматизация: 95%. Качество: низкое (только структура). [CODE BLOCK] Результат: - passports/<format>.md — авто-заполнен по структуре - adapters/<format>.py — статика из найденных файлов - autoreport.json — детальный отчёт Сканер — отправная точка, результат всё равно нужно проверить и уточнить Q6-маппинг вручную.

heading: 12.5. Path E — GitHub Actions Webhook
text: Время: 30 минут настройки, далее полностью автоматически. Автоматизация: 100%. Качество: низкое без Q6-маппинга. 1. В целевом репо создать .github/workflows/registernautilus.yml 2. При push в target-репо — repositorydispatch event в portal-репо 3. В portal-репо .github/workflows/autoupdate.yml запускает scanrepo.py + generatepassport.py + commit Плюсы: полностью автоматически. Минусы: нужны GitHub токены с правами на оба репо, Q6 всё равно требует ручной проверки.

heading: 12.6. Path Selection Guidance
text: Вариант Когда использовать ---------------------------- A Когда хорошо знаете структуру Repo и хотите high-quality B Стартовая точка для большинства новых Repos C Для Repos, которые автор хочет сам декларировать D Для быстрой первой интеграции незнакомых Repos E Для автоматической fleet-federation многих Repos Рекомендуемый путь для типового Repo: B → доработать вручную. Для federation новых Repos в будущем — C. --- --- Смотрите также: - 87-12-onboarding-paths-normative - 21-adr-003-five-onboarding-paths - 03-registry - 02-terminology --- Похожие документы: - 87-12-onboarding-paths-normative (сходство 0.87) - 87-12-onboarding-paths-normative (сходство 0.80) - 21-adr-003-five-onboarding-paths (сходство 0.17)

Compatible portal MUST предоставлять: Метод Путь Описание ----------------------- GET /api/query?q=<text>&ranked=<0\1> Поиск концептов GET /api/describe Описание всех адаптеров GET /api/health Состояние экосистемы (score 0–100)

heading: 15. Security Considerations
text: > > Источник: MHTML‑снимок Вакансии в Anthropic по кластерам - Claude (корень репозитория). Раздел диалога — Nautilus Portal Protocol v1.1 RFC, написанный совместно с Claude. --- > Источник: MHTML‑снимок Вакансии в Anthropic по кластерам - Claude (корень репозитория). Раздел диалога — Nautilus Portal Protocol v1.1 RFC, написанный совместно с Claude.

heading: 15.1. Untrusted Adapters
text: Adapters выполняются внутри Portal-процесса. Portal MUST обрабатывать adapters как untrusted code: - Timeout на каждый adapter call (RECOMMENDED 5 секунд) - Exception handling вокруг каждого call - XSS protection (html.escape) во всех user-facing rendered полях Portal SHOULD НЕ выполнять adapters из untrusted sources без review.

heading: 15.2. Private Repositories
text: Repos, содержащие чувствительные данные (legal, medical, personal), SHOULD НЕ включаться в публичную registry. Для приватных Repos RECOMMENDED: - Отдельный private-nautilus.json с explicit opt-in - Отдельный portal instance, не доступный публично - Authentication на REST API (JWT / API keys) - Нет MCP exposure без whitelist Implementation MUST различать public и private registries и не смешивать результаты без явного запроса.

heading: 15.3. MCP Exposure
text: При экспонировании Portal через MCP (см. раздел 16), implementation SHOULD: - Требовать явный whitelist Repos, доступных через MCP - Логировать queries отдельно, с возможностью очистки - Не включать private Repos в MCP responses по умолчанию Public-facing portals SHOULD реализовать rate limiting: - RECOMMENDED: 60 requests per minute per IP для /api/query - RECOMMENDED: 120 requests per minute per IP для /api/describe и /api/health

heading: 15.5. Supply Chain
text: Reference implementation использует только Python stdlib (zero external dependencies). Это RECOMMENDED для альтернативных implementations. Каждая external dependency — потенциальный supply-chain риск. --- --- Смотрите также: - 90-15-security-considerations - 11-security-considerations - 23-11-security-considerations - 06-adapter-interface --- Похожие документы: - 90-15-security-considerations (сходство 0.74) - 90-15-security-considerations (сходство 0.69) - 11-security-considerations (сходство 0.59)

MCP wrapper (portal-mcp.py в reference implementation) SHOULD экспонировать минимум 5 tools: - nautilusquery(query, repos=None) — search across ecosystem - nautiluslistrepos() — list all repos with metadata - nautilusqueryrepo(repo, query) — query single repo - nautilusconsensuscheck(concept) — consensus validation - nautilusdescribe() — ecosystem philosophy and metadata Формальная MCP-спецификация будет частью NPP v1.2 или v2.0. --- --- Смотрите также: - 91-16-mcp-extension-informative - 17-appendix-b-change-log - 13-reference-implementation - 11-relevance-ranking --- Похожие документы: - 91-16-mcp-extension-informative (сходство 0.60) - 91-16-mcp-extension-informative (сходство 0.60) - 11-relevance-ranking (сходство 0.33)

heading: 17. Versioning Policy
text: NPP следует semver: - Major (v1 → v2): breaking changes в interfaces или schemas - Minor (v1.0 → v1.1): новая функциональность, backward-compatible - Patch (v1.0.0 → v1.0.1): clarifications, typo fixes Каждый registry MUST объявлять protocolversion. Portal MUST поддерживать минимум текущую major version. v1.1 porталы MUST поддерживать v1.0 адаптеры через shim-логику (default values для новых полей типа isfallback). Для major version bump требуется: 1. RFC в Issues репо Portal с обоснованием 2. Минимум 30 дней обсуждения 3. Migration guide для существующих адаптеров 4. Compatibility shim в Portal минимум на 6 месяцев

heading: 17.4. Deprecation Policy
text: Когда поле/метод помечается deprecated: - Remains functional в текущей major version - Warning log при использовании - Удаляется только в следующем major bump --- --- Смотрите также: - 92-17-versioning-policy - 12-versioning-policy - 24-12-versioning-policy - 07-portal-entry --- Похожие документы: - 92-17-versioning-policy (сходство 0.74) - 92-17-versioning-policy (сходство 0.73) - 12-versioning-policy (сходство 0.57)

heading: 19. ADR-001: Federation over Merging
text: > > Источник: MHTML‑снимок Вакансии в Anthropic по кластерам - Claude (корень репозитория). Раздел диалога — Nautilus Portal Protocol v1.1 RFC, написанный совместно с Claude. --- > Источник: MHTML‑снимок Вакансии в Anthropic по кластерам - Claude (корень репозитория). Раздел диалога — Nautilus Portal Protocol v1.1 RFC, написанный совместно с Claude.

heading: 19. ADR-001: Federation over Merging
text: Status: Accepted (since v1.0, reaffirmed in v1.1) Context: При построении системы knowledge management встаёт выбор: заставить все данные мигрировать в единую схему (merge), либо сохранить разные native-форматы и переводить по необходимости (federation). Decision: Выбрана federation. Consequences: Positive: - Low barrier to entry: существующие Repos подключаются без рефакторинга - Authorship preserved: каждый автор работает в своей модели - Multi-angle views: один концепт может существовать в трёх Repos с тремя разными углами одновременно - Five onboarding paths: гибкость от manual до fully automated Negative: - Cross-repo queries дороже: требуется вызов всех адаптеров - Consensus нетривиален: string matching — аппроксимация, не formal mapping - Bridges — текстовые описания, не machine-executable (открытый вопрос для v2.0) Alternatives rejected: - Unified RDF/OWL ontology: слишком высокий barrier - Centralized database: нарушает local autonomy - Schema-less dump: теряется семантика angles --- --- Смотрите также: - 14-adr-001-federation-over-merging - 94-19-adr-001-federation-over-merging - 26-14-adr-001-federation-over-merging - 21-adr-003-five-onboarding-paths --- Похожие документы: - 14-adr-001-federation-over-merging (сходство 0.85) - 94-19-adr-001-federation-over-merging (сходство 0.79) - 94-19-adr-001-federation-over-merging (сходство 0.79)

parent_heading: 20. ADR-002: Q6 as First-Class Protocol Concept
heading: 20. ADR-002: Q6 as First-Class Protocol Concept
text: Status: Accepted (new in v1.1) Context: В v1.0 Q6-пространство существовало как implementation detail reference portal (pro2 → hexagrams). При росте экосистемы стало ясно, что Q6 работает как универсальная система координат для всех Repos, не только pro2. Decision: В v1.1 Q6 повышается до нормативного концепта протокола. Level 2+ адаптеры MUST обеспечивать Q6-координаты. Каждый format MUST документировать свой Q6 mapping rule. Consequences: Positive: - Унифицированная система координат для всей экосистемы - Возможность cross-format similarity queries через Hamming distance - Визуализации (Q6 map, heatmaps) работают universally - CA-class mapping даёт rich symbolic interpretation Negative: - Adapters Level 2+ вынуждены определять Q6 mapping - 64 вершин может быть мало для некоторых форматов (но достаточно для большинства) - v2.0 может понадобиться Q8 или Q12 для больших экосистем --- --- Смотрите также: - 95-20-adr-002-q6-as-first-class-protocol-concept - 21-adr-003-five-onboarding-paths - 11-relevance-ranking - 14-sdk --- Похожие документы: - 95-20-adr-002-q6-as-first-class-protocol-concept (сходство 0.70) - 95-20-adr-002-q6-as-first-class-protocol-concept (сходство 0.69) - 21-adr-003-five-onboarding-paths (сходство 0.27)

heading: 21. ADR-003: Five Onboarding Paths as Equal-Rank
text: Status: Accepted (new in v1.1) Context: В v1.0 была одна "основная" стратегия подключения (manual adapter). В процессе развития reference implementation появились 4 дополнительных пути (B–E). Возникал вопрос: какой канонический? Decision: Все 5 путей — равнорангованные. Выбор делается по контексту (раздел 12.6), не по иерархии. Consequences: Positive: - Flexibility для разных use cases - Self-declaring repos (Path C) enables federation без центральной координации - Automated paths (D, E) scale для fleet-federation Negative: - Больше документации - Users могут путаться, какой путь выбрать (mitigated via раздел 12.6 guidance) --- --- Смотрите также: - 96-21-adr-003-five-onboarding-paths-as-equal-rank - 19-adr-001-federation-over-merging - 20-adr-002-q6-first-class - 11-relevance-ranking --- Похожие документы: - 96-21-adr-003-five-onboarding-paths-as-equal-rank (сходство 0.73) - 96-21-adr-003-five-onboarding-paths-as-equal-rank (сходство 0.68) - 20-adr-002-q6-first-class (сходство 0.27)

Если у кого-то есть v1.0, они могут читать v1.1 параллельно — те же разделы говорят о том же, плюс новые. Что я добавил новое Раздел 8 — Q6 Space Normative. Это главное нововведение. В v1.0 Q6 упоминался как implementation detail. Теперь это first-class protocol concept со строгим mapping rules, normative requirement для Level 2+, и алгоритмом Q6-neighbors через Hamming. Раздел 12 — Five Onboarding Paths. Пять стратегий (A–E) из INTEGRATION.md формализованы как equal-rank выборы. Это отражает то, что на практике все пять работают, и ни одна не "главная". Раздел 13 — REST API Contract. Mandatory для compatible porталов. Три required endpoints (/api/query, /api/describe, /api/health) плюс четыре recommended. Формальные JSON schemas для responses. Раздел 14 — SDK Contract. Informative — описывает, что нужно SDK на других языках, чтобы считаться NPP-compatible. Раздел 16 — MCP Extension. Informative, но указывает, какие 5 tools минимум должна экспонировать MCP-обёртка. Это закладывает фундамент для будущего portal-mcp.py, о котором мы говорили. ADR-002 и ADR-003 — два новых architecture decisions, обосновывающих Q6 upgrade и five-paths decision. isfallback как normative field. Это критично: STATUS.md явно признаёт 88% fallback. Нормализация isfallback в протокол — честный подход, который позволяет consensus-модели различать real и fallback coverage.

Если какой-то из них ещё не создан (например, passportschema.json) — это open item, который легко заметить. Почему это важный артефакт для вас Три причины, по которым v1.1 специфично ценно: Первое — arxiv-ready. На этом документе можно построить arxiv preprint "Nautilus Portal Protocol: A Git-Based Federation Framework for Knowledge Ecosystems". Draft paper — это, по сути, reorganized abstract + sections 1–19 + метрики из 18.1 + цитирования. Первое/второе — defensible claim. ADR-001, ADR-002, ADR-003 — это formal записи ваших архитектурных решений с обоснованиями. Любой reviewer, спрашивающий "почему federation, а не centralization?" — получает ссылку на ADR-001. Это научная строгость. Третье — grant-ready. EIC Pathfinder или Horizon Europe application могут ссылаться на PORTAL-PROTOCOL.md как formal specification, которая "already has reference implementation meeting its requirements" — это сильный сигнал технической зрелости для reviewers. heading: TL;DR
text: Для критически важных документов проекта применяется трёхфазный review: 1. Вариант A создаётся независимо первым Claude-агентом (ветка 1) 2. Вариант B создаётся независимо вторым Claude-агентом (ветка 2) 3. Вариант C — ручная консолидация после сравнения A и B До достижения Фазы C оба варианта сохраняются параллельно в документе. Дубликаты секций — намеренные, не merge-conflict. Это защита от потери информации: то, что упускает один агент, может заметить другой. Механический merge ("выбрать один, удалить другой") теряет часть insights безвозвратно. --- --- Смотрите также: - 104-appendix-c-references - 122-глоссарий - 16-glossary - 18-comment-on-document --- Похожие документы: - 104-appendix-c-references (сходство 0.72) - 104-appendix-c-references (сходство 0.71) - 122-глоссарий (сходство 0.17)

heading: Executive Summary
text: The Open Knowledge Work Foundation (OKWF) proposes to build infrastructure that enables qualified individuals outside traditional employment markets to make meaningful, compensated contributions to knowledge-intensive projects through AI-coordinated specialization. We identify a systemic market failure: frontier AI companies have hundreds of unfilled senior positions (Anthropic alone had 440 open roles as of April 2026), while simultaneously, substantial talent pools remain underutilized due to geographic, regulatory, health, or life-stage constraints. This includes retired experts, disabled specialists, career-switchers, and domain experts in non-CS backgrounds. Existing staffing platforms (Deel, Toptal, Upwork, Mercor) treat these populations as transactional labor, not as dignified contributors with persistent professional identity. OKWF proposes a foundation-funded infrastructure combining: 1. Nautilus Portal Protocol — open technical substrate for federated knowledge work 2. Double-Triangle Architecture — human-AI collaboration pattern preserving individual autonomy 3. Pattern library with private instances — compounding shared knowledge without privacy loss 4. Guild-based community structure — persistent professional identity across engagements 5. Graduated participation model — entry at any capacity, progression at individual pace 6. Subsidiarity-based economic model — dignified stipend plus contribution bonuses, not charity and not employment We propose three-year pilot, initial funding €3-8M, launching in German legal/social domain (which is unusually well-suited due to existing technical infrastructure and author's direct engagement with target populations), expanding to additional domains and geographies based on empirical outcomes. This document is offered to potential foundation partners, academic collaborators, corporate sponsors, and policy stakeholders as basis for dialogue and refinement. ---

heading: 1.1. The Paradox of Abundant Demand and Underutilized Supply
text: The AI industry in 2026 exhibits an apparent paradox. Frontier labs simultaneously report unprecedented demand for senior technical talent and unprecedented labor shortages. As of April 2026: - Anthropic: 440 open positions, primarily senior roles at $320K–$405K compensation - OpenAI: estimated 600+ open positions - Google DeepMind: estimated 400+ open positions - Mistral, Cohere, Inflection, xAI: collectively 1000+ open positions Total unmet senior-level demand in AI companies alone exceeds 2,500 positions globally. Existing recruiting pipelines close perhaps 100-200 of these positions monthly across the industry. Simultaneously, substantial populations of qualified knowledge workers remain outside this hiring pipeline through no deficiency in skill or motivation. We estimate this pool contains approximately: - 500,000+ retired professionals globally with relevant technical expertise and available time, in OECD countries alone - 5M+ disabled specialists with intact intellectual capacity but modified work requirements - Unknown but substantial population of domain experts (lawyers, doctors, educators) with demonstrable AI interest but no CS credentials - Millions of geographically-dispersed skilled workers in regions without direct hire access to frontier AI companies These populations are not mutually exclusive and together represent talent resource orders of magnitude larger than current unmet demand. The failure is not of supply and demand. The failure is of matching infrastructure.

heading: 1.2. Why This Matters Beyond Economics
text: Unemployment or underemployment of qualified elders and disabled experts is not merely economic inefficiency. It produces measurable harms: - Individual harms: loss of professional identity, social isolation, declining cognitive engagement, depression - Family harms: reduced contribution to household economics, increased caregiving burden - Societal harms: loss of accumulated tacit knowledge, weakening of intergenerational transmission, reduced civic participation - AI safety harms: narrower perspective pool in AI development, reduced diversity in alignment research, concentration of AI power in demographically homogeneous groups The last point is particularly relevant for foundation missions. Beneficial AI requires diverse human input. Current pipelines systematically exclude several high-value perspective sources.

heading: 1.3. Why Now
text: Three trends converge to make this infrastructure newly feasible: First, AI capabilities (particularly LLM-based assistants like Claude, GPT-4, Gemini) crossed a threshold in 2024-2025 enabling genuine coordination of distributed knowledge work previously requiring collocation. The Model Context Protocol (MCP) released by Anthropic in late 2024 provides standardized substrate for AI-assisted workflows. Second, employment models are shifting: classic full-time permanent employment covers declining fraction of knowledge work. 36% of US workers engage in freelance/contract work in 2026, up from 27% in 2020. Foundation-funded dignified alternatives become newly viable. Third, regulatory frameworks are catching up: EU Platform Work Directive (2024), EU AI Act (2024), various national initiatives recognize digital labor as policy concern. Infrastructure built now can shape future regulation rather than adapting to it. This is not a future problem requiring speculative solutions. It is a present problem with actionable solutions that require coordinated action now. --- --- Смотрите также: - 155-1-problem-statement - 02-target-populations - 09-call-for-partnership - 153-executive-summary --- Похожие документы: - 155-1-problem-statement (сходство 0.89) - 155-1-problem-statement (сходство 0.85) - 00-abstract (сходство 0.21)

heading: 2.1. Retired Experts with Remaining Intellectual Capacity
text: Size estimate: 500K+ in OECD countries with relevant skills for knowledge work. Characteristics: - Decades of domain expertise - Reduced but significant work capacity (15-25 hours/week typical) - Modest income supplementation preferred over full salary - Value meaning, mentorship, intellectual engagement - Often excluded from tech roles due to age bias Unique value proposition from OKWF: - Dignified part-time engagement - Mentorship opportunities for younger contributors - AI assistance mitigating technical skill obsolescence - Identity continuity with professional life Example use cases: - Retired German law professor contributing to legal pattern library and junior legal writer mentorship - Former medical researcher reviewing bioinformatics code - Retired journalist leading science communication guild

heading: 2.2. Disabled Specialists with Modified Work Requirements
text: Size estimate: 5M+ in OECD countries with preserved intellectual capacity and specific support needs. Characteristics: - Technical expertise often comparable to non-disabled peers - Variable daily capacity (good days and bad days) - Need flexibility impossible in most full-time roles - Often experience employment discrimination - Frequently have direct relevant experience (e.g., disability rights advocacy) Unique value proposition from OKWF: - Asynchronous work accommodating variable capacity - No discrimination in selection (AI-assisted blind matching) - Bringing disability-informed perspective to beneficial AI - Direct expertise in accessibility research Example use cases: - Blind contributor working on screen reader AI improvements - Chronic fatigue patient contributing health AI research during good hours - Person with Sozialgericht experience (like this document's author) contributing to legal advocacy infrastructure

heading: 2.3. Career-Switchers with Non-Traditional Backgrounds
text: Size estimate: Difficult to quantify; substantial and growing. Characteristics: - Established expertise in non-CS domain (law, medicine, education, humanities) - Interest and aptitude for technical work - Often mid-career with family responsibilities - Credentials don't match AI company hiring criteria - Self-taught technical skills through coursework, projects Unique value proposition from OKWF: - Recognition of transferable expertise - Domain knowledge as differentiator, not disadvantage - Graduated progression from advisory to technical roles - Mentorship relationships with domain-specific AI teams Example use cases: - Lawyer contributing to legal AI alignment research - Medical doctor reviewing medical AI safety - Teacher designing educational AI curricula

heading: 2.4. Geographically Dispersed Skilled Workers
text: Size estimate: Millions globally outside US/UK/EU. Characteristics: - CS or technical skills comparable to hire-eligible peers - Cannot relocate due to family, legal, or preference - Existing platforms (Toptal, Upwork) treat them as commodities - Legal/tax infrastructure burden makes direct hire impractical for companies Unique value proposition from OKWF: - Foundation-handled compliance (single legal interface for contributors globally) - Persistent professional identity across engagements - Reputation portable across clients - Dignity-preserving rate structure Example use cases: - Senior engineer in Belarus contributing to AI safety research - ML researcher in Argentina working on multilingual models - Medical AI specialist in Kenya contributing to global health AI

heading: 2.5. Cross-Cutting Observations
text: These four populations overlap substantially. A retired lawyer in Belarus with chronic illness fits all four categories. Traditional hiring infrastructure treats each constraint as individual disqualifier. OKWF treats each as attribute of a unique contributor whose combination of characteristics has value. Estimated total addressable contributor pool: 10M+ globally. Active participant target in first three years: 5,000-10,000. --- --- Смотрите также: - 156-2-target-populations - 01-problem-statement - 03-why-existing-fail - 10-appendices --- Похожие документы: - 156-2-target-populations (сходство 0.88) - 156-2-target-populations (сходство 0.87) - 01-problem-statement (сходство 0.21)

heading: 3.1. Classical Employment (Full-Time)
text: Approach: Traditional W-2/permanent employment with single employer. Strengths: Stability, benefits, identity, clear accountability. Failures for target populations: - Requires relocation or strict remote eligibility - Requires full-time capacity (40+ hours/week) - Requires legal work authorization in employer jurisdiction - Hiring pipelines filter out non-traditional candidates via credentialism and interview-format bias Verdict: Fundamentally mismatched to target populations.

heading: 3.2. Freelance Platforms (Upwork, Fiverr, Toptal)
text: Approach: Marketplace matching contractors to short-term projects. Strengths: Geographic flexibility, part-time possible, lower barriers to entry. Failures for target populations: - Race-to-the-bottom pricing for knowledge workers - No persistent identity across clients - Heavy platform fees (20-30%) - Transactional relationships preclude deep engagement - Quality variable, reputation building difficult Verdict: Treats knowledge work as commodity; incompatible with dignity preservation.

heading: 3.3. Executive Contracting (Deel, Remote, Oyster, Velocity Global)
text: Approach: Employer of Record (EoR) services enabling companies to hire internationally without local entity. Strengths: Solves legal compliance for cross-border hiring. Failures for target populations: - High fees (10-15% markup) make small engagements impractical - Bureaucratic overhead unsuitable for part-time or variable capacity - No community infrastructure - Companies still need to source candidates independently Verdict: Solves one aspect (compliance) in isolation; expensive.

heading: 3.4. AI Talent Platforms (Mercor, Braintrust, A.Team)
text: Approach: AI-enhanced matching of specialized contractors to enterprise projects. Strengths: Modern infrastructure, AI-assisted matching, better UX. Failures for target populations: - Target enterprise clients with budgets for premium consultants - Selection bias toward younger, Silicon Valley-adjacent talent - No dignity-preserving structure for elders or disabled - Revenue model misaligned with non-commercial work Verdict: Better than legacy alternatives but fundamentally same commercial paradigm.

heading: 3.5. Open-Source Foundations (Apache, Linux, Python, Mozilla)
text: Approach: Non-profit foundations supporting open-source software development. Strengths: Mission-driven, community-based, enduring. Failures for target populations: - Volunteer-centric model excludes those needing income - Tech-centric, excludes non-CS domains - Prestige-driven, benefit leaders more than rank-and-file - No mechanism for serving target populations specifically Verdict: Model template is valuable; execution doesn't match target.

heading: 3.6. Government-Funded Training Programs
text: Approach: Publicly funded vocational training and placement. Strengths: Broad access, subsidized, legal protections. Failures for target populations: - Designed for young job-seekers, not elders or career-switchers - Training focuses on entry-level roles, not senior expertise - Placement into traditional employment (with its own mismatches) - Bureaucratic, slow-moving Verdict: Wrong target; different problem.

heading: 3.7. Corporate Social Responsibility Programs
text: Approach: Tech companies run inclusion programs, disability hiring, senior workforce initiatives. Strengths: Ethical intent, some funding available, aligned with ESG reporting. Failures for target populations: - Often limited to single company, small cohorts - Charity framing undignified for qualified experts - Dependent on individual company priorities (volatile) - No cross-company coordination or infrastructure Verdict: Good intent, fragmented execution, no infrastructure effect.

heading: 3.8. Summary
text: Each existing category addresses one fragment of what OKWF proposes. None combine: - Geographic flexibility + dignity preservation + community continuity + compliance solutions + AI-assisted coordination + foundation funding model The gap is not individual pieces but integration of pieces into coherent infrastructure. OKWF's proposed contribution is this integration. --- --- Смотрите также: - 157-3-why-existing-solutions-fail - 02-target-populations - 156-2-target-populations - 04-proposed-infrastructure --- Похожие документы: - 157-3-why-existing-solutions-fail (сходство 0.91) - 157-3-why-existing-solutions-fail (сходство 0.89) - 02-target-populations (сходство 0.20)

heading: 4.1. Technical Substrate: Nautilus Portal Protocol
text: Reference implementation: github.com/svend4/nautilus (v1.1) What it provides: - Federated knowledge storage across heterogeneous formats - Adapter-based access to diverse knowledge sources - Unified coordinate space (Q6) for concepts - Consensus model for conflict resolution - REST API, SDK, MCP integration - Open specification enabling alternative implementations Why Nautilus over alternatives: - Already exists and demonstrably works - Zero external dependencies (auditable, portable) - Federation-over-merging principle aligns with foundation values - Humanities extension in development - Author-maintained and open source Extensions required for OKWF: - Agent registry (AI assistants and meta-agents as first-class participants) - Task protocol (formal task objects with lifecycle) - Role protocol (first-class roles and scope) - Identity and reputation extensions Implementation timeline: 6-9 months from funding to production-ready for pilot.

heading: 4.2. Coordination Pattern: Double-Triangle Architecture
text: Reference document: THE-DOUBLE-TRIANGLE-ARCHITECTURE.md (available on request) What it provides: - Formal model for human-AI collaboration preserving human agency - Three inter-layer protocols (Human↔Assistants, Meta-agent↔Human, Assistant↔Meta-agent negotiation) - Fractal self-similarity supporting arbitrary organizational scale - Six architectural invariants ensuring dignified collaboration Why Double-Triangle for OKWF: - Explicitly designed for single contributors in distributed teams - Preserves contributor autonomy (lower triangle) - Enables coordination at scale (upper triangle) - Compatible with any AI capability level (degrades gracefully) Application to OKWF: - Each contributor = Node with personal AI assistants (lower triangle) - Guild-level coordination via meta-agent (upper triangle) - Cross-guild coordination via higher-level meta-agents (fractal) - Foundation staff provide meta-meta coordination where required

heading: 4.3. Knowledge Layer: Pattern Library with Private Instances
text: Reference document: Described in Nautilus humanities extension and Double-Triangle document What it provides: - Two-tier knowledge structure: public patterns + private instances - Three bridge types: inheritance, citation, contribution - Anonymization pipeline enabling privacy-preserving sharing - Version control and update propagation Why pattern library is critical: - Compounds knowledge across contributors - Enables specialized work without reinvention - Preserves contributor intellectual property through anonymization - Creates foundation's unique asset over time Application to OKWF: - Public patterns maintained by foundation and guilds - Private instances held by individual contributors - Anonymization pipeline operated by foundation with contributor consent - Contribution credits toward reputation and bonuses

heading: 4.4. Community Layer: Guild Structure
text: What it is: Persistent professional communities organized by specialization. Proposed initial guilds: - Legal Writing Guild — German/EU social and disability law documentation - Science Communication Guild — arxiv paper translation and summarization - Medical Documentation Guild — healthcare system documentation and translation - Technical Writing Guild — software documentation, educational materials - Code Review Guild — open-source code review for specified projects - Research Synthesis Guild — literature review and synthesis Guild functions: - Skill validation and graduation - Mentorship (senior contributors guide juniors) - Quality assurance (peer review of outputs) - Project formation (teams assemble for specific engagements) - Community governance (guild-internal decisions) Graduation structure: - Level 0: Onboarding quests (2-4 weeks) - Level 1: Simple structured tasks (3-6 months) - Level 2: Specialized work (6-18 months) - Level 3: Project participation (ongoing) - Level 4: Project leadership (elected or merit-based) - Level 5: Guild leadership (elected)

heading: 4.5. Economic Layer: Subsidiarity-Based Compensation
text: Principles: - Dignified baseline: all active contributors receive stipend meeting minimum threshold - Contribution bonuses: measurable high-impact work generates additional compensation - Long-term incentives: reputation and credentials translate into tangible benefits - No full-replacement income: contributors retain other life activities Proposed structure: - Base stipend: €500-1500/month for active contributors (depending on region cost-of-living) - Project bonuses: €500-5000/project for specific deliverables - Revenue sharing: when projects serve paying clients, substantial share flows to contributors - Milestone awards: guild-level recognition with tangible credentials Why subsidiarity is right model: - Below full-salary level preserves other life activities (elders, disabled, caregivers) - Above charity level preserves dignity and agency - Predictable baseline allows planning - Variable bonus allows excellence recognition Funding sources: - Corporate sponsorships (core unrestricted funds) - Grant funding (project-specific) - Client payments (commercial projects) - Foundation endowment (eventual self-sustaining core)

heading: 4.6. Legal and Compliance Layer
text: What it provides: - Single contractual interface for contributors (across jurisdictions) - Tax compliance infrastructure - GDPR-compliant data handling - Dispute resolution process - Intellectual property framework Mechanism: - OKWF foundation provides EoR-equivalent services - AI-assisted compliance automation reduces costs 10x vs. existing platforms - Modular legal templates for different engagement types - Clear IP ownership model (default: contributor retains rights; project-specific exceptions with transparent terms)

heading: 4.7. Integration
text: All six layers integrate: - Contributors log in (community identity), pick up tasks (guild structure, pattern library), execute work (Nautilus substrate + Double-Triangle with assistants), receive compensation (economic layer handled by legal layer). Foundation staff operate: - Technical infrastructure - Initial pattern curation - Legal and compliance - Funding relationships Community governance operates: - Guild-level quality gates - Mentorship programs - Strategic input to foundation leadership --- --- Смотрите также: - 158-4-proposed-infrastructure - 04-nautilus-portal-substrate - 05-economic-model - 06-four-deployment-domains --- Похожие документы: - 158-4-proposed-infrastructure (сходство 0.93) - 158-4-proposed-infrastructure (сходство 0.93) - 04-nautilus-portal-substrate (сходство 0.23)

heading: 5.1. Budget Philosophy
text: OKWF is not and will not become a for-profit entity. Operational sustainability comes from: 1. Core endowment from foundational corporate partners and foundation grants (€3-8M initial, growing to €50M+ target) 2. Grant funding for specific initiatives and research (€1-3M annually) 3. Earned revenue from commercial projects routed through platform (growing to majority of operational funding over 5-7 years) 4. Contributor fees: explicit zero (0%) platform fees for contributors in dignity-preserving roles

heading: 5.2. Three-Year Pilot Budget (Estimated)
text: Category Year 1 Year 2 Year 3 ---------------------------------- Core staff (5-8 people) €600K €900K €1.2M Infrastructure development €400K €300K €300K Contributor stipends (growing cohort) €300K €1.2M €2.5M Contributor bonuses €100K €400K €1M Legal and compliance €150K €200K €300K Administrative and governance €100K €150K €200K Community events and outreach €100K €150K €250K Reserve and contingency €250K €500K €750K Total annual €2M €3.8M €6.5M Three-year total: €12.3M

heading: 5.3. Funding Strategy
text: Phase 1 (months 1-6): Secure anchor partnership (€2-3M commitment over 3 years) from one AI company. Most likely candidate: Anthropic Public Benefit team, which has explicit mission alignment and existing programs. Phase 2 (months 3-9, overlapping): Secure secondary partnerships (€500K-1M each) from 2-3 additional sources: - Complementary AI company (Mistral, Cohere) - Foundation grant (Mozilla, Ford, Wikimedia) - EU program grant (EIC Pathfinder, Horizon Europe) Phase 3 (months 6-18): Launch commercial project revenue stream. Target: €200K-500K in Year 1 commercial revenue scaling to €2M+ in Year 3. Phase 4 (Year 2-3): Develop endowment through success-contingent additional sponsorships.

heading: 5.4. Contributor Economics
text: Representative contributor profiles: Contributor A: Retired German law professor, 20 hours/week - Base stipend: €800/month - Typical bonuses: €500-1500/month (from 1-2 completed projects) - Annual total: €15,600-27,600 - Other income: pension, partial Contributor B: Disabled senior engineer (like document author), 15 hours/week - Base stipend: €900/month (reflecting Germany cost-of-living) - Typical bonuses: €800-2000/month (from specialized technical work) - Annual total: €20,400-34,800 - Other income: disability pension, does not conflict with foundation work structure Contributor C: Domain expert in Belarus, 25 hours/week - Base stipend: €600/month (local cost-of-living adjusted) - Typical bonuses: €1000-3000/month (from technical work) - Annual total: €19,200-43,200 - Local purchasing power equivalent to €40K-90K in Germany Contributor D: Career-switcher PhD teacher in Spain, 10 hours/week - Base stipend: €500/month (limited engagement) - Typical bonuses: €300-800/month - Annual total: €9,600-15,600 - Supplements existing income, explores transition These profiles are illustrative. Actual compensation reflects engagement level, impact, skill, and region. Foundation commits to minimum dignity threshold; upward is merit-based. --- --- Смотрите также: - 159-5-economic-model - 07-phased-rollout - 04-proposed-infrastructure - 09-call-for-partnership --- Похожие документы: - 159-5-economic-model (сходство 0.87) - 159-5-economic-model (сходство 0.86) - 07-phased-rollout (сходство 0.23)

Ethical Red Lines --- > Источник: MHTML‑снимок Вакансии в Anthropic по кластерам - Claude (корень репозитория). Раздел диалога — paper «Open Knowledge Work Foundation Concept Document».

heading: 6.1. Governance Structure
text: Board of Directors (7 members): - 2 corporate partners (rotating) - 2 foundation representatives (Mozilla-type) - 2 contributor representatives (elected by contributors) - 1 independent (academic or public policy) Executive team (3-5 people): - Executive Director - Technical Director - Community Director - Legal Director Guild councils: Each guild has elected 3-person council governing internal affairs. Contributor assembly: Annual gathering of all contributors with binding decisional authority on specified matters (stipend minimums, dignity standards, guild boundaries).

heading: 6.2. Key Ethical Commitments
text: Commitment 1: No contributor will be charged fees or subjected to rent-seeking. Foundation takes 0% from contributor earnings. Commitment 2: Contributor intellectual property remains with contributor by default. Specific projects may transfer rights with transparent, negotiated terms. Commitment 3: Anonymization of contributed patterns is voluntary; contributor always retains attribution right. Commitment 4: No algorithmic management. AI assists coordination but final decisions about contributors remain with humans. Commitment 5: Private data (contributors' personal circumstances, health information) remains with contributor. Foundation handles compliance through delegation, not data accumulation. Commitment 6: Transparent operations. Financials, decisions, pattern library, infrastructure all publicly auditable. Commitment 7: Graceful exit. Contributors can leave at any time. Their contributions, reputation, and public patterns remain with foundation. Private data exported on request.

heading: 6.3. Conflict of Interest Management
text: Corporate partners fund foundation but cannot: - Direct specific contributor assignment - Access contributor private data - Modify pattern library priorities - Override governance decisions Contributors' sponsors contribute capital but receive only: - Anonymized patterns (public) - Transparency reports (aggregated) - Brand association (opt-in) - First-look invitations to collaborative projects (not guarantees)

heading: 6.4. Regulatory Compliance
text: GDPR (EU): Foundation operates as data controller for minimal required personal data. Pattern library governed by GDPR Article 89 (scientific/statistical research) where appropriate. Contributor data sovereignty preserved. Employment law: Foundation-contributor relationship structured as contractor/grantee, not employment. Compliance with each jurisdiction's requirements (consulting tax law firm during setup). AI Act (EU): Foundation's use of AI in matching, coordination, quality assurance subject to AI Act transparency requirements. Proactive compliance and documentation. Disability rights (all jurisdictions): Infrastructure explicitly designed for accessibility. Contributor accommodations built into platform rather than retrofitted.

heading: 6.5. Ethical Red Lines
text: Foundation will not: - Support projects intended for military use - Accept funding contingent on compromising commitment 1-7 - Use contributor data for AI training without explicit consent - Share contributor private data except as legally required - Allow algorithmic decisions to replace human judgment on dignified issues --- --- Смотрите также: - 160-6-governance-and-ethics - 04-proposed-infrastructure - 09-call-for-partnership - 10-appendices --- Похожие документы: - 160-6-governance-and-ethics (сходство 0.85) - 160-6-governance-and-ethics (сходство 0.85) - 09-call-for-partnership (сходство 0.18)

heading: 7.1. Phase 0: Foundation and Funding (Months 1-6)
text: Key activities: - Establish legal entity (non-profit foundation in Germany or similar jurisdiction) - Secure anchor corporate funding commitment - Assemble core team (Executive Director, Technical Director, Community Director) - Finalize pilot design with anchor partner - Refine technical infrastructure specifications Success criteria: - €2-3M committed funding - Core team hired - Legal entity established - Pilot design agreed with partner Risk mitigations: - If anchor commitment fails: pivot to foundation-only funding with delayed, smaller pilot - If core team assembly slow: partner with established entity (e.g., Mozilla Foundation) as fiscal sponsor temporarily

heading: 7.2. Phase 1: Infrastructure and First Cohort (Months 6-18)
text: Key activities: - Build production-ready technical infrastructure (Nautilus-based) - Recruit first 50 contributors, primarily in legal writing guild - Execute 5-10 pilot projects (mix of internal, partner-funded, open source) - Establish pattern library initial contents - Document outcomes and learnings Success criteria: - 50 active contributors engaged - 5-10 completed pilot projects with measurable quality outcomes - Working pattern library with 100+ public patterns - Contributor satisfaction survey: >75% positive - Public output: 1-2 published papers/articles on learnings Risk mitigations: - If contributor recruitment slow: increase outreach investment; partner with existing networks (LinkedIn groups, disability advocacy organizations) - If project outcomes disappointing: iterate on selection criteria and support structures

heading: 7.3. Phase 2: Scale and Diversification (Months 18-36)
text: Key activities: - Expand to 500+ contributors across 3-5 guilds - Launch commercial project revenue stream - Establish second guild (potentially science communication or medical documentation) - Pilot in second geographic region beyond Germany - Develop API and integrations for third-party applications Success criteria: - 500+ active contributors - €500K+ annual commercial revenue - 3-5 fully functional guilds - Operational in 2+ countries - Break-even on commercial projects Risk mitigations: - If commercial revenue slow: pivot to more grant funding reliance temporarily - If guild expansion difficult: prioritize depth in first guild over breadth

heading: 7.4. Phase 3: Consolidation and Self-Sustaining Operations
text: (Years 3-5) Key activities: - Expand to 5000+ contributors - Develop endowment and reduce funding dependency - Formalize international partnerships - Influence policy on AI-assisted distributed work - Publish methodological outcomes for external replication Success criteria: - 5000+ active contributors globally - Self-sustaining core operations (non-grant funding covers baseline) - 10+ active research partnerships - Policy impact measurable - Replicable methodology documented

heading: 7.5. Long-Term Vision (Years 5-10)
text: OKWF evolves into trusted infrastructure for distributed expert work globally. Multiple foundations and corporate partners sustain operations. Contributors span every continent. Specialization extends across most knowledge domains. Secondary institutions emerge following OKWF methodology with domain-specific focus (OKWF Medical, OKWF Education, OKWF Legal, etc.). Collaborative federation of such institutions through common protocols (Nautilus-based) creates network effect in knowledge work infrastructure. Long-term impact: transformation of knowledge work from classical employment-centered model to distributed, dignified, AI-coordinated participation accessible to all qualified individuals regardless of traditional constraints. --- --- Смотрите также: - 161-7-phased-rollout-plan - 05-economic-model - 09-phased-rollout - 09-call-for-partnership --- Похожие документы: - 161-7-phased-rollout-plan (сходство 0.88) - 161-7-phased-rollout-plan (сходство 0.86) - 05-economic-model (сходство 0.23)

heading: 8.1. Risk: Insufficient Demand from Target Contributor Population
text: Probability: Medium Impact: High (foundational failure) Evidence of demand: - Substantial LinkedIn/professional network discussions about underemployment of elder professionals - Active disability advocacy groups seeking employment infrastructure - Growing remote-work populations seeking dignified engagement - Successful precedents in related spaces (Kaggle, Stack Overflow, open-source communities) Mitigations: - Aggressive outreach during Phase 1 recruitment - Partnership with existing advocacy organizations - Focused pilot in high-density population area - Built-in pivot capacity (can consolidate to single guild if needed)

heading: 8.2. Risk: Insufficient Demand from Clients/Sponsors
text: Probability: Low (given 2500+ unfilled positions at frontier AI companies) Impact: High (funding dependency) Evidence of demand: - Frontier AI companies explicitly discuss shortage - Anthropic Beneficial Deployment team is natural home for such initiative - EU regulatory trajectory favors such infrastructure - Corporate ESG pressures for inclusive hiring Mitigations: - Anchor partner committed before launch - Multiple partnership types explored in parallel - Hybrid funding model (corporate + foundation + grants) - Strong pipeline of grants submissions maintained always

heading: 8.3. Risk: Quality Outcomes Insufficient
text: Probability: Medium (especially Year 1) Impact: High (reputation) Evidence that quality achievable: - Existing open-source communities produce high-quality work - AI-assistance dramatically reduces variance - Guild-based mentorship structures proven elsewhere - Reference implementation (Nautilus) demonstrates quality achievable by single individual Mitigations: - Rigorous selection in Phase 1 - Explicit quality gates at each guild level - Peer review processes - AI-assisted consistency checking - External quality auditing - Willingness to restart pilots if quality insufficient

heading: 8.4. Risk: Regulatory Challenges
text: Probability: Low-Medium Impact: Medium Risk sources: - Employment law reclassifying contributors as employees - GDPR enforcement challenges with anonymization pipeline - AI Act regulatory requirements - Tax law differences across jurisdictions Mitigations: - Explicit regulatory counsel from early stages - Proactive engagement with regulators - Conservative approach (pay more in taxes, compliance overhead rather than risk) - Legal infrastructure for rapid pivot if regulations shift

heading: 8.5. Risk: Organizational Capacity
text: Probability: Medium Impact: Medium-High Risk sources: - Small organization ambitious scope - Founder-operator burnout (including author-of-document) - Single points of failure in leadership - Coordination overhead at scale Mitigations: - Realistic hiring of core team (5-8 people, not 3) - Co-leadership model (Executive Director supported by technical, community leads) - Clear succession planning from launch - Explicit boundary-setting for founders - Regular sustainability assessments

heading: 8.6. Risk: Adversarial Participants
text: Probability: Low-Medium (grows with scale) Impact: Medium Risk sources: - Contributors gaming the system - Clients extracting value without compensation - Pattern library corruption - AI-generated work passing as human Mitigations: - Reputation systems resistant to gaming - Multiple quality gates - Sponsor vetting - Transparent operations enabling community oversight - Ongoing monitoring and response protocols

heading: 8.7. Comprehensive Risk Assessment
text: On balance, OKWF faces typical challenges of a new foundation with novel scope. None of identified risks are unique or categorically concerning. Established mitigations and contingency planning address most scenarios. Residual risk level: moderate, within acceptable range for mission-driven foundation launch. --- --- Смотрите также: - 162-8-risk-analysis - 07-phased-rollout - 04-proposed-infrastructure - 09-call-for-partnership --- Похожие документы: - 162-8-risk-analysis (сходство 0.91) - 162-8-risk-analysis (сходство 0.89) - 07-phased-rollout (сходство 0.19)

heading: 9.1. For AI Companies (Anthropic, Mistral, OpenAI, Google, Microsoft)
text: What we ask: - Core partnership funding commitment (€1-3M over 3 years) - Integration of OKWF contributors into specific projects - Technical infrastructure support (compute, AI access for contributors) - Mentorship from senior staff to contributor community - Governance board participation What you receive: - Direct access to qualified talent pool outside your hiring pipeline - Demonstrable commitment to beneficial AI values - Research insights on distributed AI-assisted work - Public recognition as foundational partner - First-look on collaborative projects Natural fit partners: - Anthropic Public Benefit team: explicit mission alignment - Google.org: established corporate philanthropy - Mozilla: commitment to open source and inclusive tech

heading: 9.2. For Foundations (Mozilla, Ford, Wikimedia, Hewlett)
text: What we ask: - Multi-year grant commitment (€500K-2M) - Expertise in mission-driven infrastructure - Introductions to aligned stakeholders - Advocacy for OKWF model in broader foundation ecosystem What you receive: - Scalable impact on intellectual participation - Alignment with AI safety and inclusion missions - Data and learnings from novel infrastructure experiment - Complement existing programs rather than duplicate

heading: 9.3. For Academic and Research Partners (Universities, Think Tanks)
text: What we ask: - Research collaboration on methodology and outcomes - Student and researcher contributor recruitment - Policy and governance expertise - Academic credibility through endorsement What you receive: - Access to novel research environment - Publication opportunities - Grant collaboration possibilities - Impact beyond traditional academic boundaries

heading: 9.4. For Policy Organizations (EU Commission, National Governments, OECD)
text: What we ask: - Regulatory guidance and dialogue - Recognition of experimental status - Potential grant funding (EU Horizon, national programs) - Advocacy in policy development affecting distributed work What you receive: - Concrete evidence for policy development - Early warning on emerging regulatory needs - Test case for AI-assisted work frameworks - Constituency of beneficiaries

heading: 9.5. For Potential Contributors
text: What we ask: - Willingness to participate in early cohorts - Feedback on infrastructure and processes - Mentorship to newer contributors - Community building What you receive: - Dignified, meaningful engagement - Income proportional to contribution - Persistent professional identity - Community and mentorship - Opportunity to shape infrastructure design --- --- Смотрите также: - 163-9-call-for-partnership - 07-phased-rollout - 05-economic-model - 10-appendices --- Похожие документы: - 163-9-call-for-partnership (сходство 0.86) - 163-9-call-for-partnership (сходство 0.85) - 07-phased-rollout (сходство 0.21)

heading: Appendix C: Founder Biography
text: Author (svend4) is a software architect and legal advocate based in Dresden, Germany, working on Sozialgericht proceedings (cases S 6 SO 58/26 ER and S 7 SO 99/25) involving Eingliederungshilfe, Persönliches Budget, and disability rights. Personal circumstance (disability, geographic constraints, active legal proceedings) makes author ideal type-specimen target contributor for OKWF, providing authentic credibility and mission alignment. text: Target: Anthropic Beneficial Deployments team Why Anthropic specifically: - Public Benefit Corporation structure aligns with foundation mission - Existing programs for beneficial deployments suggest appetite for infrastructure investment - 440+ open positions demonstrates scale of unmet demand - Claude and Claude Code are natural infrastructure substrate - Anthropic's values on safety, inclusion, beneficial AI map directly to OKWF mission Proposed engagement structure: - 3-year commitment at €1-1.5M per year - Governance board seat (1 of 7) - Technical integration (Claude access for contributors at sustainable rate) - Staff mentorship for contributor community - Co-authored case studies on outcomes Return on investment for Anthropic: - Talent pipeline that current hiring pipeline cannot reach - Demonstrable alignment between stated values and operational commitments - Research insights on distributed AI-assisted work patterns - Goodwill with disability, elder, and geographic diversity populations - Potential to influence policy on AI-assisted work models Timeline to formal proposal: - Initial outreach: next 30 days (author can draft warm approach to Beneficial Deployments team lead) - First exploratory conversation: within 60 days - Formal proposal submission: within 120 days - Decision target: 180-240 days
